search_text: Deep Bidirectional Language-Knowledge Graph Pretraining 深度双向语言-知识图预训练 Pretraining a language model (LM) on text has been shown to help various downstream NLP tasks. Recent works show that a knowledge graph (KG) can complement text data, offering structured background knowledge that provides a useful scaffold for reasoning. However, these works are not pretrained to learn a deep fusion of the two modalities at scale, limiting the potential to acquire fully joint representations of text and KG. Here we propose DRAGON (Deep Bidirectional Language-Knowledge Graph Pretraining), a self-supervised approach to pretraining a deeply joint language-knowledge foundation model from text and KG at scale. Specifically, our model takes pairs of text segments and relevant KG subgraphs as input and bidirectionally fuses information from both modalities. We pretrain this model by unifying two self-supervised reasoning tasks, masked language modeling and KG link prediction. DRAGON outperforms existing LM and LM+KG models on diverse downstream tasks including question answering across general and biomedical domains, with +5% absolute gain on average. In particular, DRAGON achieves notable performance on complex reasoning about language and knowledge (+10% on questions involving long contexts or multi-step reasoning) and low-resource QA (+8% on OBQA and RiddleSense), and new state-of-the-art results on various BioNLP tasks. Our code and trained models are available at https://github.com/michiyasunaga/dragon. 事实证明，在文本上预训练语言模型 (LM) 有助于各种下游 NLP 任务。最近的工作表明，知识图（KG）可以补充文本数据，提供结构化的背景知识，为推理提供有用的支架。然而，这些作品并未经过预先训练来大规模学习两种模式的深度融合，从而限制了获得文本和 KG 完全联合表示的潜力。在这里，我们提出了 DRAGON（深度双向语言知识图预训练），这是一种自监督方法，用于大规模地从文本和知识图谱中预训练深度联合的语言知识基础模型。具体来说，我们的模型将文本片段对和相关 KG 子图作为输入，并双向融合来自两种模式的信息。我们通过统一两个自监督推理任务（掩码语言建模和 KG 链接预测）来预训练该模型。 DRAGON 在各种下游任务（包括一般和生物医学领域的问答）上优于现有 LM 和 LM+KG 模型，平均绝对增益 +5%。特别是，DRAGON 在有关语言和知识的复杂推理（涉及长上下文或多步骤推理的问题上+10%）和低资源 QA（在 OBQA 和 RiddleSense 上+8%）方面取得了显着的性能，并在各种 BioNLP 任务上取得了最新的最新结果。我们的代码和经过训练的模型可在 https://github.com/michiyasunaga/dragon 上获取。 本文提出DRAGON模型，通过深度双向融合文本与知识图谱（KG）进行预训练，提升多任务表现。 Michihiro Yasunaga,Antoine Bosselut,Hongyu Ren,Xikun Zhang,Christopher D Manning,Percy Liang,Jure Leskovec cs.CL,cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Jung Yeon Park,Lawson L. S. Wong
title: Robust Imitation of a Few Demonstrations with a Backwards Model
title_zh: 使用向后模型对一些演示进行鲁棒模仿
comment: Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS) 2022
comments: Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS) 2022
abstract: Behavior cloning of expert demonstrations can speed up learning optimal policies in a more sample-efficient way over reinforcement learning. However, the policy cannot extrapolate well to unseen states outside of the demonstration data, creating covariate shift (agent drifting away from demonstrations) and compounding errors. In this work, we tackle this issue by extending the region of attraction around the demonstrations so that the agent can learn how to get back onto the demonstrated trajectories if it veers off-course. We train a generative backwards dynamics model and generate short imagined trajectories from states in the demonstrations. By imitating both demonstrations and these model rollouts, the agent learns the demonstrated paths and how to get back onto these paths. With optimal or near-optimal demonstrations, the learned policy will be both optimal and robust to deviations, with a wider region of attraction. On continuous control domains, we evaluate the robustness when starting from different initial states unseen in the demonstration data. While both our method and other imitation learning baselines can successfully solve the tasks for initial states in the training distribution, our method exhibits considerably more robustness to different initial states.
summary: Behavior cloning of expert demonstrations can speed up learning optimal policies in a more sample-efficient way over reinforcement learning. However, the policy cannot extrapolate well to unseen states outside of the demonstration data, creating covariate shift (agent drifting away from demonstrations) and compounding errors. In this work, we tackle this issue by extending the region of attraction around the demonstrations so that the agent can learn how to get back onto the demonstrated trajectories if it veers off-course. We train a generative backwards dynamics model and generate short imagined trajectories from states in the demonstrations. By imitating both demonstrations and these model rollouts, the agent learns the demonstrated paths and how to get back onto these paths. With optimal or near-optimal demonstrations, the learned policy will be both optimal and robust to deviations, with a wider region of attraction. On continuous control domains, we evaluate the robustness when starting from different initial states unseen in the demonstration data. While both our method and other imitation learning baselines can successfully solve the tasks for initial states in the training distribution, our method exhibits considerably more robustness to different initial states.
abstract_zh: 与强化学习相比，专家演示的行为克隆可以以更有效的样本方式加速学习最优策略。然而，该政策无法很好地推断到示范数据之外未见过的状态，从而产生协变量偏移（代理偏离示范）并加剧错误。在这项工作中，我们通过扩展演示周围的吸引区域来解决这个问题，以便代理能够学习如何在偏离路线时回到演示的轨迹。我们训练一个生成向后动力学模型，并从演示中的状态生成短的想象轨迹。通过模仿演示和这些模型的推出，代理可以学习演示的路径以及如何返回这些路径。通过最优或接近最优的演示，学习到的策略将是最优的且对偏差具有鲁棒性，具有更广泛的吸引力区域。在连续控制域上，我们评估从演示数据中未见的不同初始状态开始时的鲁棒性。虽然我们的方法和其他模仿学习基线都可以成功解决训练分布中初始状态的任务，但我们的方法对不同的初始状态表现出相当强的鲁棒性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种通过逆向模型增强模仿学习鲁棒性的方法，解决智能体在未见过状态下的偏差问题。
AI.motivation: 传统模仿学习在面对未见过的状态时易产生协变量偏移和误差累积，导致策略失效，需扩展示范数据的吸引域以提升鲁棒性。
AI.method: 训练生成逆向动力学模型，从示范状态生成虚拟轨迹，并通过模仿示范数据与模型生成轨迹联合训练策略。
AI.result: 在连续控制任务中，该方法在未见过的初始状态下表现更稳健，显著优于其他模仿学习基线方法。
AI.conclusion: 逆向模型辅助的模仿学习能有效扩展策略的吸引域，使智能体在偏离示范轨迹时具备恢复能力，提升对初始状态分布外样本的鲁棒性。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出一种通过逆向模型增强模仿学习鲁棒性的方法，解决智能体在未见过状态下的偏差问题。
ai_content.motivation: 传统模仿学习在面对未见过的状态时易产生协变量偏移和误差累积，导致策略失效，需扩展示范数据的吸引域以提升鲁棒性。
ai_content.method: 训练生成逆向动力学模型，从示范状态生成虚拟轨迹，并通过模仿示范数据与模型生成轨迹联合训练策略。
ai_content.result: 在连续控制任务中，该方法在未见过的初始状态下表现更稳健，显著优于其他模仿学习基线方法。
ai_content.conclusion: 逆向模型辅助的模仿学习能有效扩展策略的吸引域，使智能体在偏离示范轨迹时具备恢复能力，提升对初始状态分布外样本的鲁棒性。

search_text: Robust Imitation of a Few Demonstrations with a Backwards Model 使用向后模型对一些演示进行鲁棒模仿 Behavior cloning of expert demonstrations can speed up learning optimal policies in a more sample-efficient way over reinforcement learning. However, the policy cannot extrapolate well to unseen states outside of the demonstration data, creating covariate shift (agent drifting away from demonstrations) and compounding errors. In this work, we tackle this issue by extending the region of attraction around the demonstrations so that the agent can learn how to get back onto the demonstrated trajectories if it veers off-course. We train a generative backwards dynamics model and generate short imagined trajectories from states in the demonstrations. By imitating both demonstrations and these model rollouts, the agent learns the demonstrated paths and how to get back onto these paths. With optimal or near-optimal demonstrations, the learned policy will be both optimal and robust to deviations, with a wider region of attraction. On continuous control domains, we evaluate the robustness when starting from different initial states unseen in the demonstration data. While both our method and other imitation learning baselines can successfully solve the tasks for initial states in the training distribution, our method exhibits considerably more robustness to different initial states. 与强化学习相比，专家演示的行为克隆可以以更有效的样本方式加速学习最优策略。然而，该政策无法很好地推断到示范数据之外未见过的状态，从而产生协变量偏移（代理偏离示范）并加剧错误。在这项工作中，我们通过扩展演示周围的吸引区域来解决这个问题，以便代理能够学习如何在偏离路线时回到演示的轨迹。我们训练一个生成向后动力学模型，并从演示中的状态生成短的想象轨迹。通过模仿演示和这些模型的推出，代理可以学习演示的路径以及如何返回这些路径。通过最优或接近最优的演示，学习到的策略将是最优的且对偏差具有鲁棒性，具有更广泛的吸引力区域。在连续控制域上，我们评估从演示数据中未见的不同初始状态开始时的鲁棒性。虽然我们的方法和其他模仿学习基线都可以成功解决训练分布中初始状态的任务，但我们的方法对不同的初始状态表现出相当强的鲁棒性。 本文提出一种通过逆向模型增强模仿学习鲁棒性的方法，解决智能体在未见过状态下的偏差问题。 Jung Yeon Park,Lawson L. S. Wong cs.LG,cs.AI

authors_text: Sean Kinahan,Julie Liss,Visar Berisha
title: TorchDIVA: An Extensible Computational Model of Speech Production built on an Open-Source Machine Learning Library
title_zh: TorchDIVA：基于开源机器学习库构建的可扩展语音生成计算模型
abstract: The DIVA model is a computational model of speech motor control that combines a simulation of the brain regions responsible for speech production with a model of the human vocal tract. The model is currently implemented in Matlab Simulink; however, this is less than ideal as most of the development in speech technology research is done in Python. This means there is a wealth of machine learning tools which are freely available in the Python ecosystem that cannot be easily integrated with DIVA. We present TorchDIVA, a full rebuild of DIVA in Python using PyTorch tensors. DIVA source code was directly translated from Matlab to Python, and built-in Simulink signal blocks were implemented from scratch. After implementation, the accuracy of each module was evaluated via systematic block-by-block validation. The TorchDIVA model is shown to produce outputs that closely match those of the original DIVA model, with a negligible difference between the two. We additionally present an example of the extensibility of TorchDIVA as a research platform. Speech quality enhancement in TorchDIVA is achieved through an integration with an existing PyTorch generative vocoder called DiffWave. A modified DiffWave mel-spectrum upsampler was trained on human speech waveforms and conditioned on the TorchDIVA speech production. The results indicate improved speech quality metrics in the DiffWave-enhanced output as compared to the baseline. This enhancement would have been difficult or impossible to accomplish in the original Matlab implementation. This proof-of-concept demonstrates the value TorchDIVA will bring to the research community. Researchers can download the new implementation at: https://github.com/skinahan/DIVA_PyTorch
summary: The DIVA model is a computational model of speech motor control that combines a simulation of the brain regions responsible for speech production with a model of the human vocal tract. The model is currently implemented in Matlab Simulink; however, this is less than ideal as most of the development in speech technology research is done in Python. This means there is a wealth of machine learning tools which are freely available in the Python ecosystem that cannot be easily integrated with DIVA. We present TorchDIVA, a full rebuild of DIVA in Python using PyTorch tensors. DIVA source code was directly translated from Matlab to Python, and built-in Simulink signal blocks were implemented from scratch. After implementation, the accuracy of each module was evaluated via systematic block-by-block validation. The TorchDIVA model is shown to produce outputs that closely match those of the original DIVA model, with a negligible difference between the two. We additionally present an example of the extensibility of TorchDIVA as a research platform. Speech quality enhancement in TorchDIVA is achieved through an integration with an existing PyTorch generative vocoder called DiffWave. A modified DiffWave mel-spectrum upsampler was trained on human speech waveforms and conditioned on the TorchDIVA speech production. The results indicate improved speech quality metrics in the DiffWave-enhanced output as compared to the baseline. This enhancement would have been difficult or impossible to accomplish in the original Matlab implementation. This proof-of-concept demonstrates the value TorchDIVA will bring to the research community. Researchers can download the new implementation at: https://github.com/skinahan/DIVA_PyTorch
abstract_zh: DIVA 模型是一种语音运动控制的计算模型，它将负责语音产生的大脑区域的模拟与人类声道的模型相结合。该模型目前在Matlab Simulink中实现；然而，这并不理想，因为语音技术研究的大部分开发都是在 Python 中完成的。这意味着 Python 生态系统中有大量免费提供的机器学习工具无法轻松与 DIVA 集成。我们推出了 TorchDIVA，它是使用 PyTorch 张量在 Python 中完全重建 DIVA 的版本。 DIVA 源代码直接从 Matlab 翻译为 Python，并从头开始实现内置 Simulink 信号模块。实施后，通过系统的逐块验证来评估每个模块的准确性。 TorchDIVA 模型所产生的输出与原始 DIVA 模型的输出非常匹配，两者之间的差异可以忽略不计。我们还提供了一个 TorchDIVA 作为研究平台的可扩展性示例。 TorchDIVA 中的语音质量增强是通过与现有的 PyTorch 生成声码器（称为 DiffWave）集成来实现的。改进的 DiffWave 梅尔频谱上采样器在人类语音波形上进行训练，并以 TorchDIVA 语音生成为条件。结果表明，与基线相比，DiffWave 增强输出中的语音质量指标有所改善。在最初的 Matlab 实现中，这种增强是很难或不可能完成的。这一概念验证证明了 TorchDIVA 将为研究界带来的价值。研究人员可以在以下位置下载新的实现：https://github.com/skinahan/DIVA_PyTorch

AI.tldr: 本文提出TorchDIVA，一个基于PyTorch的可扩展语音生产计算模型，旨在替代原有Matlab Simulink实现的DIVA模型。
AI.motivation: 原有DIVA模型依赖Matlab Simulink，难以与Python生态中的机器学习工具集成，限制了研究扩展性。
AI.method: 将DIVA源代码直接移植至Python并用PyTorch实现，从零构建Simulink信号模块，通过模块化验证确保准确性，并集成DiffWave生成式声码器提升语音质量。
AI.result: TorchDIVA输出与原DIVA模型高度一致，DiffWave增强后语音质量指标显著优于基线，证明其扩展性优势。
AI.conclusion: TorchDIVA为语音研究提供了更灵活的Python平台，便于集成先进机器学习工具，推动未来研究发展。

authors_text: Liang Jin,Shixuan Gu,Donglai Wei,Jason Ken Adhinarta,Kaiming Kuang,Yongjie Jessica Zhang,Hanspeter Pfister,Bingbing Ni,Jiancheng Yang,Ming Li
title: RibSeg v2: A Large-scale Benchmark for Rib Labeling and Anatomical Centerline Extraction
title_zh: RibSeg v2：肋骨标记和解剖中心线提取的大型基准
abstract: Automatic rib labeling and anatomical centerline extraction are common prerequisites for various clinical applications. Prior studies either use in-house datasets that are inaccessible to communities, or focus on rib segmentation that neglects the clinical significance of rib labeling. To address these issues, we extend our prior dataset (RibSeg) on the binary rib segmentation task to a comprehensive benchmark, named RibSeg v2, with 660 CT scans (15,466 individual ribs in total) and annotations manually inspected by experts for rib labeling and anatomical centerline extraction. Based on the RibSeg v2, we develop a pipeline including deep learning-based methods for rib labeling, and a skeletonization-based method for centerline extraction. To improve computational efficiency, we propose a sparse point cloud representation of CT scans and compare it with standard dense voxel grids. Moreover, we design and analyze evaluation metrics to address the key challenges of each task. Our dataset, code, and model are available online to facilitate open research at https://github.com/M3DV/RibSeg
summary: Automatic rib labeling and anatomical centerline extraction are common prerequisites for various clinical applications. Prior studies either use in-house datasets that are inaccessible to communities, or focus on rib segmentation that neglects the clinical significance of rib labeling. To address these issues, we extend our prior dataset (RibSeg) on the binary rib segmentation task to a comprehensive benchmark, named RibSeg v2, with 660 CT scans (15,466 individual ribs in total) and annotations manually inspected by experts for rib labeling and anatomical centerline extraction. Based on the RibSeg v2, we develop a pipeline including deep learning-based methods for rib labeling, and a skeletonization-based method for centerline extraction. To improve computational efficiency, we propose a sparse point cloud representation of CT scans and compare it with standard dense voxel grids. Moreover, we design and analyze evaluation metrics to address the key challenges of each task. Our dataset, code, and model are available online to facilitate open research at https://github.com/M3DV/RibSeg
abstract_zh: 自动肋骨标记和解剖中心线提取是各种临床应用的常见先决条件。先前的研究要么使用社区无法访问的内部数据集，要么专注于肋骨分割，而忽略了肋骨标记的临床意义。为了解决这些问题，我们将之前的二元肋骨分割任务数据集 (RibSeg) 扩展为一个名为 RibSeg v2 的综合基准，其中包含 660 个 CT 扫描（总共 15,466 根肋骨）以及专家手动检查的注释，以进行肋骨标记和解剖中心线提取。基于 RibSeg v2，我们开发了一个管道，包括基于深度学习的肋骨标记方法和基于骨架化的中心线提取方法。为了提高计算效率，我们提出了 CT 扫描的稀疏点云表示，并将其与标准密集体素网格进行比较。此外，我们设计和分析评估指标以解决每项任务的关键挑战。我们的数据集、代码和模型可在线获取，以促进开放研究：https://github.com/M3DV/RibSeg

AI.tldr: 本文提出一个大规模基准测试RibSeg v2，用于肋骨标签和解剖中心线提取，并开发了相关算法与评估指标。
AI.motivation: 现有研究或使用封闭数据集，或仅关注分割而忽略临床意义的肋骨标签，缺乏全面的公开基准测试。
AI.method: 扩展原始数据集为包含660个CT扫描的RibSeg v2，采用深度学习进行肋骨标签，骨架化方法提取中心线，提出稀疏点云表示并设计任务特定评估指标。
AI.result: 构建了专家标注的大型数据集，开发了高效算法，验证了稀疏点云优势，开源代码与模型促进后续研究。
AI.conclusion: 通过开放数据集和方法，推动肋骨分析领域临床应用与算法研究的进展。

method: 扩展原始数据集为包含660个CT扫描的RibSeg v2，采用深度学习进行肋骨标签，骨架化方法提取中心线，提出稀疏点云表示并设计任务特定评估指标。
result: 构建了专家标注的大型数据集，开发了高效算法，验证了稀疏点云优势，开源代码与模型促进后续研究。
conclusion: 通过开放数据集和方法，推动肋骨分析领域临床应用与算法研究的进展。
search_text: RibSeg v2: A Large-scale Benchmark for Rib Labeling and Anatomical Centerline Extraction RibSeg v2：肋骨标记和解剖中心线提取的大型基准 Automatic rib labeling and anatomical centerline extraction are common prerequisites for various clinical applications. Prior studies either use in-house datasets that are inaccessible to communities, or focus on rib segmentation that neglects the clinical significance of rib labeling. To address these issues, we extend our prior dataset (RibSeg) on the binary rib segmentation task to a comprehensive benchmark, named RibSeg v2, with 660 CT scans (15,466 individual ribs in total) and annotations manually inspected by experts for rib labeling and anatomical centerline extraction. Based on the RibSeg v2, we develop a pipeline including deep learning-based methods for rib labeling, and a skeletonization-based method for centerline extraction. To improve computational efficiency, we propose a sparse point cloud representation of CT scans and compare it with standard dense voxel grids. Moreover, we design and analyze evaluation metrics to address the key challenges of each task. Our dataset, code, and model are available online to facilitate open research at https://github.com/M3DV/RibSeg 自动肋骨标记和解剖中心线提取是各种临床应用的常见先决条件。先前的研究要么使用社区无法访问的内部数据集，要么专注于肋骨分割，而忽略了肋骨标记的临床意义。为了解决这些问题，我们将之前的二元肋骨分割任务数据集 (RibSeg) 扩展为一个名为 RibSeg v2 的综合基准，其中包含 660 个 CT 扫描（总共 15,466 根肋骨）以及专家手动检查的注释，以进行肋骨标记和解剖中心线提取。基于 RibSeg v2，我们开发了一个管道，包括基于深度学习的肋骨标记方法和基于骨架化的中心线提取方法。为了提高计算效率，我们提出了 CT 扫描的稀疏点云表示，并将其与标准密集体素网格进行比较。此外，我们设计和分析评估指标以解决每项任务的关键挑战。我们的数据集、代码和模型可在线获取，以促进开放研究：https://github.com/M3DV/RibSeg 本文提出一个大规模基准测试RibSeg v2，用于肋骨标签和解剖中心线提取，并开发了相关算法与评估指标。 Liang Jin,Shixuan Gu,Donglai Wei,Jason Ken Adhinarta,Kaiming Kuang,Yongjie Jessica Zhang,Hanspeter Pfister,Bingbing Ni,Jiancheng Yang,Ming Li eess.IV,cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Alex Mei,Anisha Kabir,Sharon Levy,Melanie Subbiah,Emily Allaway,John Judge,Desmond Patton,Bruce Bimber,Kathleen McKeown,William Yang Wang
title: Mitigating Covertly Unsafe Text within Natural Language Systems
title_zh: 减少自然语言系统中隐蔽的不安全文本
comment: In Findings of the 2022 Conference on Empirical Methods in Natural Language Processing
comments: In Findings of the 2022 Conference on Empirical Methods in Natural Language Processing
abstract: An increasingly prevalent problem for intelligent technologies is text safety, as uncontrolled systems may generate recommendations to their users that lead to injury or life-threatening consequences. However, the degree of explicitness of a generated statement that can cause physical harm varies. In this paper, we distinguish types of text that can lead to physical harm and establish one particularly underexplored category: covertly unsafe text. Then, we further break down this category with respect to the system's information and discuss solutions to mitigate the generation of text in each of these subcategories. Ultimately, our work defines the problem of covertly unsafe language that causes physical harm and argues that this subtle yet dangerous issue needs to be prioritized by stakeholders and regulators. We highlight mitigation strategies to inspire future researchers to tackle this challenging problem and help improve safety within smart systems.
summary: An increasingly prevalent problem for intelligent technologies is text safety, as uncontrolled systems may generate recommendations to their users that lead to injury or life-threatening consequences. However, the degree of explicitness of a generated statement that can cause physical harm varies. In this paper, we distinguish types of text that can lead to physical harm and establish one particularly underexplored category: covertly unsafe text. Then, we further break down this category with respect to the system's information and discuss solutions to mitigate the generation of text in each of these subcategories. Ultimately, our work defines the problem of covertly unsafe language that causes physical harm and argues that this subtle yet dangerous issue needs to be prioritized by stakeholders and regulators. We highlight mitigation strategies to inspire future researchers to tackle this challenging problem and help improve safety within smart systems.
abstract_zh: 智能技术日益普遍的问题是文本安全，因为不受控制的系统可能会向用户生成建议，从而导致受伤或危及生命的后果。然而，生成的可能导致人身伤害的语句的明确程度各不相同。在本文中，我们区分了可能导致人身伤害的文本类型，并建立了一个特别未被充分探索的类别：隐蔽的不安全文本。然后，我们根据系统信息进一步细分此类别，并讨论减少每个子类别中文本生成的解决方案。最终，我们的工作定义了导致人身伤害的隐蔽不安全语言问题，并认为利益相关者和监管机构需要优先考虑这一微妙但危险的问题。我们重点介绍缓解策略，以激励未来的研究人员解决这一具有挑战性的问题，并帮助提高智能系统的安全性。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在识别并缓解自然语言系统中可能引发物理伤害的隐蔽不安全文本问题。
AI.motivation: 智能系统生成的文本可能引发物理伤害，而隐蔽的不安全文本这一类别尚未被充分研究，需引起重视。
AI.method: 通过区分有害文本类型，重点分析隐蔽不安全文本的子类，并提出相应的缓解策略。
AI.result: 明确了隐蔽不安全语言的定义，提出了针对不同子类的缓解方法，并强调了其重要性。
AI.conclusion: 隐蔽不安全文本虽隐晦但危险，需相关方和监管机构优先处理，并推动未来研究以提高智能系统的安全性。

authors_text: Jinghao Zhou,Li Dong,Zhe Gan,Lijuan Wang,Furu Wei
title: Non-Contrastive Learning Meets Language-Image Pre-Training
title_zh: 非对比学习与语言图像预训练的结合
abstract: Contrastive language-image pre-training (CLIP) serves as a de-facto standard to align images and texts. Nonetheless, the loose correlation between images and texts of web-crawled data renders the contrastive objective data inefficient and craving for a large training batch size. In this work, we explore the validity of non-contrastive language-image pre-training (nCLIP), and study whether nice properties exhibited in visual self-supervised models can emerge. We empirically observe that the non-contrastive objective nourishes representation learning while sufficiently underperforming under zero-shot recognition. Based on the above study, we further introduce xCLIP, a multi-tasking framework combining CLIP and nCLIP, and show that nCLIP aids CLIP in enhancing feature semantics. The synergy between two objectives lets xCLIP enjoy the best of both worlds: superior performance in both zero-shot transfer and representation learning. Systematic evaluation is conducted spanning a wide variety of downstream tasks including zero-shot classification, out-of-domain classification, retrieval, visual representation learning, and textual representation learning, showcasing a consistent performance gain and validating the effectiveness of xCLIP.
summary: Contrastive language-image pre-training (CLIP) serves as a de-facto standard to align images and texts. Nonetheless, the loose correlation between images and texts of web-crawled data renders the contrastive objective data inefficient and craving for a large training batch size. In this work, we explore the validity of non-contrastive language-image pre-training (nCLIP), and study whether nice properties exhibited in visual self-supervised models can emerge. We empirically observe that the non-contrastive objective nourishes representation learning while sufficiently underperforming under zero-shot recognition. Based on the above study, we further introduce xCLIP, a multi-tasking framework combining CLIP and nCLIP, and show that nCLIP aids CLIP in enhancing feature semantics. The synergy between two objectives lets xCLIP enjoy the best of both worlds: superior performance in both zero-shot transfer and representation learning. Systematic evaluation is conducted spanning a wide variety of downstream tasks including zero-shot classification, out-of-domain classification, retrieval, visual representation learning, and textual representation learning, showcasing a consistent performance gain and validating the effectiveness of xCLIP.
abstract_zh: 对比语言图像预训练（CLIP）是对齐图像和文本的事实上的标准。尽管如此，网络爬取数据的图像和文本之间的松散相关性使得对比客观数据效率低下，并且需要大的训练批量。在这项工作中，我们探讨了非对比语言图像预训练（nCLIP）的有效性，并研究视觉自监督模型中是否会出现良好的特性。我们凭经验观察到，非对比目标滋养了表征学习，但在零样本识别下却表现不佳。基于上述研究，我们进一步介绍了 xCLIP，一个结合 CLIP 和 nCLIP 的多任务框架，并表明 nCLIP 帮助 CLIP 增强特征语义。两个目标之间的协同作用让 xCLIP 享受两全其美：零样本迁移和表示学习方面的卓越性能。对各种下游任务进行系统评估，包括零样本分类、域外分类、检索、视觉表示学习和文本表示学习，展示了一致的性能增益并验证了 xCLIP 的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文探讨了非对比语言-图像预训练（nCLIP）的有效性，并提出了一种结合对比和非对比目标的多任务框架xCLIP。
AI.motivation: 对比语言-图像预训练（CLIP）在对齐图像和文本方面表现优异，但由于网络爬取数据中的图像和文本之间关联较弱，导致其数据效率低下且需要较大的训练批次。本文旨在研究非对比学习是否能在这种场景中带来更好的表示学习效果。
AI.method: 作者提出了非对比语言-图像预训练（nCLIP），并进一步引入了结合CLIP和nCLIP的多任务框架xCLIP，以利用两者的协同效应。
AI.result: 系统评估表明，xCLIP在零样本迁移、域外分类、检索、视觉和文本表示学习等多个下游任务中均表现出色，验证了其有效性。
AI.conclusion: 结合对比和非对比目标的xCLIP框架能够实现零样本迁移和表示学习的双重优势，为语言-图像预训练提供了新的思路。

authors_text: Yuhong Li,Tianle Cai,Yi Zhang,Deming Chen,Debadeepta Dey
title: What Makes Convolutional Models Great on Long Sequence Modeling?
title_zh: 是什么让卷积模型在长序列建模方面表现出色？
comment: The code is available at https://github.com/ctlllll/SGConv
comments: The code is available at https://github.com/ctlllll/SGConv
abstract: Convolutional models have been widely used in multiple domains. However, most existing models only use local convolution, making the model unable to handle long-range dependency efficiently. Attention overcomes this problem by aggregating global information but also makes the computational complexity quadratic to the sequence length. Recently, Gu et al. [2021] proposed a model called S4 inspired by the state space model. S4 can be efficiently implemented as a global convolutional model whose kernel size equals the input sequence length. S4 can model much longer sequences than Transformers and achieve significant gains over SoTA on several long-range tasks. Despite its empirical success, S4 is involved. It requires sophisticated parameterization and initialization schemes. As a result, S4 is less intuitive and hard to use. Here we aim to demystify S4 and extract basic principles that contribute to the success of S4 as a global convolutional model. We focus on the structure of the convolution kernel and identify two critical but intuitive principles enjoyed by S4 that are sufficient to make up an effective global convolutional model: 1) The parameterization of the convolutional kernel needs to be efficient in the sense that the number of parameters should scale sub-linearly with sequence length. 2) The kernel needs to satisfy a decaying structure that the weights for convolving with closer neighbors are larger than the more distant ones. Based on the two principles, we propose a simple yet effective convolutional model called Structured Global Convolution (SGConv). SGConv exhibits strong empirical performance over several tasks: 1) With faster speed, SGConv surpasses S4 on Long Range Arena and Speech Command datasets. 2) When plugging SGConv into standard language and vision models, it shows the potential to improve both efficiency and performance.
summary: Convolutional models have been widely used in multiple domains. However, most existing models only use local convolution, making the model unable to handle long-range dependency efficiently. Attention overcomes this problem by aggregating global information but also makes the computational complexity quadratic to the sequence length. Recently, Gu et al. [2021] proposed a model called S4 inspired by the state space model. S4 can be efficiently implemented as a global convolutional model whose kernel size equals the input sequence length. S4 can model much longer sequences than Transformers and achieve significant gains over SoTA on several long-range tasks. Despite its empirical success, S4 is involved. It requires sophisticated parameterization and initialization schemes. As a result, S4 is less intuitive and hard to use. Here we aim to demystify S4 and extract basic principles that contribute to the success of S4 as a global convolutional model. We focus on the structure of the convolution kernel and identify two critical but intuitive principles enjoyed by S4 that are sufficient to make up an effective global convolutional model: 1) The parameterization of the convolutional kernel needs to be efficient in the sense that the number of parameters should scale sub-linearly with sequence length. 2) The kernel needs to satisfy a decaying structure that the weights for convolving with closer neighbors are larger than the more distant ones. Based on the two principles, we propose a simple yet effective convolutional model called Structured Global Convolution (SGConv). SGConv exhibits strong empirical performance over several tasks: 1) With faster speed, SGConv surpasses S4 on Long Range Arena and Speech Command datasets. 2) When plugging SGConv into standard language and vision models, it shows the potential to improve both efficiency and performance.
abstract_zh: 卷积模型已广泛应用于多个领域。然而，大多数现有模型仅使用局部卷积，使得模型无法有效地处理长程依赖。注意力通过聚合全局信息克服了这个问题，但也使计算复杂度与序列长度成二次方。最近，顾等人。 [2021] 受到状态空间模型的启发，提出了一个名为 S4 的模型。 S4 可以有效地实现为全局卷积模型，其内核大小等于输入序列长度。 S4 可以模拟比 Transformer 更长的序列，并且在一些远程任务上比 SoTA 取得了显着的进步。尽管 S4 取得了实证上的成功，但它也参与其中。它需要复杂的参数化和初始化方案。因此，S4 不太直观且难以使用。在这里，我们的目标是揭开 S4 的神秘面纱，并提取有助于 S4 作为全局卷积模型取得成功的基本原理。我们关注卷积核的结构，并确定 S4 所享有的两个关键但直观的原则，足以构成有效的全局卷积模型：1）卷积核的参数化需要高效，因为参数的数量应随序列长度呈亚线性缩放。 2）内核需要满足一个衰减结构，即与较近的邻居进行卷积的权重大于与较远的邻居进行卷积的权重。基于这两个原则，我们提出了一种简单而有效的卷积模型，称为结构化全局卷积（SGConv）。 SGConv 在多项任务中表现出强大的经验性能：1）凭借更快的速度，SGConv 在 Long Range Arena 和 Speech Command 数据集上超越了 S4。 2）当将SGConv插入标准语言和视觉模型时，它显示出提高效率和性能的潜力。

AI.tldr: 本文分析了S4模型在长序列建模中的成功因素，并提出基于两个关键原理的结构化全局卷积模型SGConv。
AI.motivation: 现有卷积模型难以处理长距离依赖，而S4虽有效但参数复杂且不易使用，需提炼其核心原理以简化实现。
AI.method: 通过分析S4的卷积核结构，提出两个关键原则：参数数量需亚线性增长，且卷积核权重需满足衰减结构。基于此设计SGConv模型。
AI.result: SGConv在Long Range Arena和Speech Command数据集上超越S4，且在语言和视觉模型中提升效率与性能。
AI.conclusion: 结构化全局卷积的两个原则是实现高效长序列建模的关键，SGConv为现有模型提供了更优的替代方案。

authors_text: Carolina Higuera,Siyuan Dong,Byron Boots,Mustafa Mukadam
title: Neural Contact Fields: Tracking Extrinsic Contact with Tactile Sensing
title_zh: 神经接触场：通过触觉感知跟踪外部接触
comment: 2023 International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA)
comments: 2023 International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA)
abstract: We present Neural Contact Fields, a method that brings together neural fields and tactile sensing to address the problem of tracking extrinsic contact between object and environment. Knowing where the external contact occurs is a first step towards methods that can actively control it in facilitating downstream manipulation tasks. Prior work for localizing environmental contacts typically assume a contact type (e.g. point or line), does not capture contact/no-contact transitions, and only works with basic geometric-shaped objects. Neural Contact Fields are the first method that can track arbitrary multi-modal extrinsic contacts without making any assumptions about the contact type. Our key insight is to estimate the probability of contact for any 3D point in the latent space of object shapes, given vision-based tactile inputs that sense the local motion resulting from the external contact. In experiments, we find that Neural Contact Fields are able to localize multiple contact patches without making any assumptions about the geometry of the contact, and capture contact/no-contact transitions for known categories of objects with unseen shapes in unseen environment configurations. In addition to Neural Contact Fields, we also release our YCB-Extrinsic-Contact dataset of simulated extrinsic contact interactions to enable further research in this area. Project page: https://github.com/carolinahiguera/NCF
summary: We present Neural Contact Fields, a method that brings together neural fields and tactile sensing to address the problem of tracking extrinsic contact between object and environment. Knowing where the external contact occurs is a first step towards methods that can actively control it in facilitating downstream manipulation tasks. Prior work for localizing environmental contacts typically assume a contact type (e.g. point or line), does not capture contact/no-contact transitions, and only works with basic geometric-shaped objects. Neural Contact Fields are the first method that can track arbitrary multi-modal extrinsic contacts without making any assumptions about the contact type. Our key insight is to estimate the probability of contact for any 3D point in the latent space of object shapes, given vision-based tactile inputs that sense the local motion resulting from the external contact. In experiments, we find that Neural Contact Fields are able to localize multiple contact patches without making any assumptions about the geometry of the contact, and capture contact/no-contact transitions for known categories of objects with unseen shapes in unseen environment configurations. In addition to Neural Contact Fields, we also release our YCB-Extrinsic-Contact dataset of simulated extrinsic contact interactions to enable further research in this area. Project page: https://github.com/carolinahiguera/NCF
abstract_zh: 我们提出了神经接触场，一种将神经场和触觉感知结合在一起的方法，以解决跟踪物体和环境之间的外部接触的问题。了解外部接触发生的位置是寻找可以主动控制外部接触以促进下游操作任务的方法的第一步。先前用于本地化环境接触的工作通常假设接触类型（例如点或线），不捕获接触/非接触过渡，并且仅适用于基本几何形状的物体。神经接触场是第一种可以跟踪任意多模态外部接触而无需对接触类型做出任何假设的方法。我们的主要见解是，在基于视觉的触觉输入感知外部接触产生的局部运动的情况下，估计对象形状的潜在空间中任何 3D 点的接触概率。在实验中，我们发现神经接触场能够定位多个接触斑块，而无需对接触的几何形状做出任何假设，并捕获未见环境配置中具有未见形状的已知物体类别的接触/非接触过渡。除了神经接触场之外，我们还发布了模拟外在接触相互作用的 YCB-Extrinsic-Contact 数据集，以促进该领域的进一步研究。项目页面：https://github.com/carolinahiguera/NCF

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种结合神经场和触觉传感的方法，用于跟踪物体与环境之间的外在接触。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在定位环境接触时通常假设接触类型（如点或线），无法捕捉接触/非接触转换，且仅适用于基础几何形状物体，限制了复杂场景下的应用。
AI.method: 通过估计物体形状潜在空间中任意3D点的接触概率，结合基于视觉的触觉输入感知外部接触引起的局部运动，实现无需假设接触类型即可跟踪多模态外在接触。
AI.result: 实验表明，该方法能无几何假设定位多个接触区域，并捕捉已知类别物体在未知形状和环境配置下的接触/非接触转换，同时发布了YCB-Extrinsic-Contact数据集。
AI.conclusion: Neural Contact Fields是首个无需接触类型假设的多模态外在接触跟踪方法，为复杂环境下的触觉感知与操控任务提供了新思路，并通过公开数据集推动领域研究。

authors_text: Nobuaki Aoki,Yasumasa Namba
title: 3rd Place Solution for Google Universal Image Embedding
title_zh: Google 通用图像嵌入解决方案第三名
abstract: This paper presents the 3rd place solution to the Google Universal Image Embedding Competition on Kaggle. We use ViT-H/14 from OpenCLIP for the backbone of ArcFace, and trained in 2 stage. 1st stage is done with freezed backbone, and 2nd stage is whole model training. We achieve 0.692 mean Precision @5 on private leaderboard. Code available at https://github.com/YasumasaNamba/google-universal-image-embedding
summary: This paper presents the 3rd place solution to the Google Universal Image Embedding Competition on Kaggle. We use ViT-H/14 from OpenCLIP for the backbone of ArcFace, and trained in 2 stage. 1st stage is done with freezed backbone, and 2nd stage is whole model training. We achieve 0.692 mean Precision @5 on private leaderboard. Code available at https://github.com/YasumasaNamba/google-universal-image-embedding
abstract_zh: 本文介绍了 Kaggle 上 Google 通用图像嵌入竞赛的第三名解决方案。我们使用 OpenCLIP 的 ViT-H/14 作为 ArcFace 的主干，并分 2 阶段进行训练。第一阶段是用冻结的骨干网完成的，第二阶段是整个模型的训练。我们在私人排行榜上实现了 0.692 平均精度 @5。代码可在 https://github.com/YasumasaNamba/google-universal-image-embedding 获取

AI.tldr: 本文介绍了在Kaggle的Google通用图像嵌入竞赛中获得第三名的解决方案，旨在通过优化图像嵌入模型提升图像检索或分类性能。
AI.motivation: 现有图像嵌入方法在通用场景下的表现存在不足，需通过分阶段训练策略提升模型适应性与精度。
AI.method: 采用ViT-H/14作为ArcFace主干网络，分两阶段训练：第一阶段冻结主干网络进行预训练，第二阶段全模型微调。
AI.result: 在私有排行榜上达到0.692的平均Precision@5，代码已开源至GitHub。

authors_text: Khondker Fariha Hossain,Sharif Amit Kamran,Prithul Sarker,Philip Pavilionis,Isayas Adhanom,Nicholas Murray,Alireza Tavakkoli
title: Virtual-Reality based Vestibular Ocular Motor Screening for Concussion Detection using Machine-Learning
title_zh: 基于虚拟现实的前庭眼运动筛查，利用机器学习进行脑震荡检测
comment: Accepted in 17th International Symposium on Visual Computing,2022
comments: Accepted in 17th International Symposium on Visual Computing,2022
abstract: Sport-related concussion (SRC) depends on sensory information from visual, vestibular, and somatosensory systems. At the same time, the current clinical administration of Vestibular/Ocular Motor Screening (VOMS) is subjective and deviates among administrators. Therefore, for the assessment and management of concussion detection, standardization is required to lower the risk of injury and increase the validation among clinicians. With the advancement of technology, virtual reality (VR) can be utilized to advance the standardization of the VOMS, increasing the accuracy of testing administration and decreasing overall false positive rates. In this paper, we experimented with multiple machine learning methods to detect SRC on VR-generated data using VOMS. In our observation, the data generated from VR for smooth pursuit (SP) and the Visual Motion Sensitivity (VMS) tests are highly reliable for concussion detection. Furthermore, we train and evaluate these models, both qualitatively and quantitatively. Our findings show these models can reach high true-positive-rates of around 99.9 percent of symptom provocation on the VR stimuli-based VOMS vs. current clinical manual VOMS.
summary: Sport-related concussion (SRC) depends on sensory information from visual, vestibular, and somatosensory systems. At the same time, the current clinical administration of Vestibular/Ocular Motor Screening (VOMS) is subjective and deviates among administrators. Therefore, for the assessment and management of concussion detection, standardization is required to lower the risk of injury and increase the validation among clinicians. With the advancement of technology, virtual reality (VR) can be utilized to advance the standardization of the VOMS, increasing the accuracy of testing administration and decreasing overall false positive rates. In this paper, we experimented with multiple machine learning methods to detect SRC on VR-generated data using VOMS. In our observation, the data generated from VR for smooth pursuit (SP) and the Visual Motion Sensitivity (VMS) tests are highly reliable for concussion detection. Furthermore, we train and evaluate these models, both qualitatively and quantitatively. Our findings show these models can reach high true-positive-rates of around 99.9 percent of symptom provocation on the VR stimuli-based VOMS vs. current clinical manual VOMS.
abstract_zh: 运动相关脑震荡 (SRC) 取决于视觉、前庭和体感系统的感觉信息。与此同时，目前前庭/眼部运动筛查（VOMS）的临床管理存在主观性，并且管理人员之间存在偏差。因此，对于脑震荡检测的评估和管理，需要标准化以降低受伤风险并增加临床医生的验证。随着技术的进步，虚拟现实 (VR) 可用于推进 VOMS 的标准化，提高测试管理的准确性并降低总体误报率。在本文中，我们尝试了多种机器学习方法，使用 VOMS 检测 VR 生成的数据上的 SRC。根据我们的观察，VR 平滑追踪 (SP) 和视觉运动灵敏度 (VMS) 测试生成的数据对于脑震荡检测来说非常可靠。此外，我们定性和定量地训练和评估这些模型。我们的研究结果表明，与当前的临床手动 VOMS 相比，这些模型在基于 VR 刺激的 VOMS 上的症状诱发率可以达到约 99.9% 的高真阳性率。

AI.tldr: 本文提出基于虚拟现实（VR）和机器学习的前庭眼动测试（VOMS）方法，用于提高运动相关脑震荡（SRC）检测的标准化和准确性。
AI.motivation: 当前临床VOMS评估依赖主观判断，存在评估者间差异，需通过标准化手段降低误诊率并提升检测可靠性。
AI.method: 利用VR生成平滑追踪（SP）和视觉运动敏感性（VMS）测试数据，结合多种机器学习模型进行训练与评估。
AI.result: 模型在VR刺激的VOMS测试中达到约99.9%的真阳性率，显著优于传统手动VOMS方法。
AI.conclusion: VR结合机器学习可提供标准化、高精度的脑震荡检测方案，为临床诊断提供可靠技术支撑。

authors_text: Alberto Alvarez,Jose Font,Julian Togelius
title: Story Designer: Towards a Mixed-Initiative Tool to Create Narrative Structures
title_zh: 故事设计师：采用混合主动工具来创建叙事结构
comment: 9 pages, Accepted and to appear in Proceedings of the 17th International Conference on the Foundations of Digital Games (FDG), 2022
comments: 9 pages, Accepted and to appear in Proceedings of the 17th International Conference on the Foundations of Digital Games (FDG), 2022
abstract: Narratives are a predominant part of games, and their design poses challenges when identifying, encoding, interpreting, evaluating, and generating them. One way to address this would be to approach narrative design in a more abstract layer, such as narrative structures. This paper presents Story Designer, a mixed-initiative co-creative narrative structure tool built on top of the Evolutionary Dungeon Designer (EDD) that uses tropes, narrative conventions found across many media types, to design these structures. Story Designer uses tropes as building blocks for narrative designers to compose complete narrative structures by interconnecting them in graph structures called narrative graphs. Our mixed-initiative approach lets designers manually create their narrative graphs and feeds an underlying evolutionary algorithm with those, creating quality-diverse suggestions using MAP-Elites. Suggestions are visually represented for designers to compare and evaluate and can then be incorporated into the design for further manual editions. At the same time, we use the levels designed within EDD as constraints for the narrative structure, intertwining both level design and narrative. We evaluate the impact of these constraints and the system's adaptability and expressiveness, resulting in a potential tool to create narrative structures combining level design aspects with narrative.
summary: Narratives are a predominant part of games, and their design poses challenges when identifying, encoding, interpreting, evaluating, and generating them. One way to address this would be to approach narrative design in a more abstract layer, such as narrative structures. This paper presents Story Designer, a mixed-initiative co-creative narrative structure tool built on top of the Evolutionary Dungeon Designer (EDD) that uses tropes, narrative conventions found across many media types, to design these structures. Story Designer uses tropes as building blocks for narrative designers to compose complete narrative structures by interconnecting them in graph structures called narrative graphs. Our mixed-initiative approach lets designers manually create their narrative graphs and feeds an underlying evolutionary algorithm with those, creating quality-diverse suggestions using MAP-Elites. Suggestions are visually represented for designers to compare and evaluate and can then be incorporated into the design for further manual editions. At the same time, we use the levels designed within EDD as constraints for the narrative structure, intertwining both level design and narrative. We evaluate the impact of these constraints and the system's adaptability and expressiveness, resulting in a potential tool to create narrative structures combining level design aspects with narrative.
abstract_zh: 叙事是游戏的主要部分，其设计在识别、编码、解释、评估和生成它们时提出了挑战。解决这个问题的一种方法是在更抽象的层面上进行叙事设计，例如叙事结构。本文介绍了 Story Designer，这是一种建立在 Evolutionary Dungeon Designer (EDD) 之上的混合主动共同创意叙事结构工具，它使用在许多媒体类型中发现的比喻和叙事惯例来设计这些结构。故事设计器使用比喻作为叙事设计者的构建块，通过将它们以称为叙事图的图形结构互连来组成完整的叙事结构。我们的混合主动方法让设计师手动创建他们的叙述图，并用这些图提供底层进化算法，使用 MAP-Elites 创建质量多样化的建议。建议以视觉方式呈现，供设计人员进行比较和评估，然后可以将其纳入设计中以供进一步的手册版本使用。同时，我们使用EDD中设计的关卡作为叙事结构的约束，将关卡设计和叙事交织在一起。我们评估这些约束的影响以及系统的适应性和表现力，从而形成一个潜在的工具来创建将关卡设计方面与叙事相结合的叙事结构。

AI.tldr: 本文提出Story Designer工具，通过混合主动方法结合叙事结构与关卡设计，利用叙事套路生成多样化叙事方案。
AI.motivation: 传统叙事设计面临复杂性挑战，需抽象化叙事结构并融合关卡设计约束以提高效率与表现力。
AI.method: 基于EDD框架，以叙事套路为构建块生成叙事图，采用MAP-Elites算法提供多样化建议，并将关卡设计作为约束条件进行协同优化。
AI.result: 验证了约束条件对系统适应性的影响，展示了工具在叙事结构生成与关卡设计结合方面的有效性。
AI.conclusion: Story Designer为叙事设计提供了混合主动工具，通过算法生成与人工编辑结合，实现了叙事与关卡设计的协同创作。

Suggestions are visually represented for designers to compare and evaluate and can then be incorporated into the design for further manual editions. At the same time, we use the levels designed within EDD as constraints for the narrative structure, intertwining both level design and narrative. We evaluate the impact of these constraints and the system's adaptability and expressiveness, resulting in a potential tool to create narrative structures combining level design aspects with narrative. 叙事是游戏的主要部分，其设计在识别、编码、解释、评估和生成它们时提出了挑战。解决这个问题的一种方法是在更抽象的层面上进行叙事设计，例如叙事结构。本文介绍了 Story Designer，这是一种建立在 Evolutionary Dungeon Designer (EDD) 之上的混合主动共同创意叙事结构工具，它使用在许多媒体类型中发现的比喻和叙事惯例来设计这些结构。故事设计器使用比喻作为叙事设计者的构建块，通过将它们以称为叙事图的图形结构互连来组成完整的叙事结构。我们的混合主动方法让设计师手动创建他们的叙述图，并用这些图提供底层进化算法，使用 MAP-Elites 创建质量多样化的建议。建议以视觉方式呈现，供设计人员进行比较和评估，然后可以将其纳入设计中以供进一步的手册版本使用。同时，我们使用EDD中设计的关卡作为叙事结构的约束，将关卡设计和叙事交织在一起。我们评估这些约束的影响以及系统的适应性和表现力，从而形成一个潜在的工具来创建将关卡设计方面与叙事相结合的叙事结构。 本文提出Story Designer工具，通过混合主动方法结合叙事结构与关卡设计，利用叙事套路生成多样化叙事方案。 Alberto Alvarez,Jose Font,Julian Togelius cs.HC,cs.AI

authors_text: Javeria Shabbir,M. Zeshan Alam,M. Umair Mukati
title: Learning Texture Transformer Network for Light Field Super-Resolution
title_zh: 学习用于光场超分辨率的纹理变换器网络
comment: European Conference on Visual Media Production (CVMP) 2022 short paper
comments: European Conference on Visual Media Production (CVMP) 2022 short paper
abstract: Hand-held light field cameras suffer from low spatial resolution due to the inherent spatio-angular tradeoff. In this paper, we propose a method to improve the spatial resolution of light field images with the aid of the Texture Transformer Network (TTSR). The proposed method consists of three modules: the first module produces an all-in focus high-resolution perspective image which serves as a reference image for the second module, i.e. TTSR, which in turn produces a high-resolution light field. The last module refines the spatial resolution by imposing a light field prior. The results demonstrate around 4 dB to 6 dB PSNR gain over a bicubically resized light field image
summary: Hand-held light field cameras suffer from low spatial resolution due to the inherent spatio-angular tradeoff. In this paper, we propose a method to improve the spatial resolution of light field images with the aid of the Texture Transformer Network (TTSR). The proposed method consists of three modules: the first module produces an all-in focus high-resolution perspective image which serves as a reference image for the second module, i.e. TTSR, which in turn produces a high-resolution light field. The last module refines the spatial resolution by imposing a light field prior. The results demonstrate around 4 dB to 6 dB PSNR gain over a bicubically resized light field image
abstract_zh: 由于固有的空间角度权衡，手持式光场相机面临空间分辨率低的问题。在本文中，我们提出了一种借助纹理变换器网络（TTSR）来提高光场图像空间分辨率的方法。该方法由三个模块组成：第一个模块产生全焦点高分辨率透视图像，作为第二个模块（即 TTSR）的参考图像，后者又产生高分辨率光场。最后一个模块通过施加光场先验来细化空间分辨率。结果表明，在双三次调整大小的光场图像上，PSNR 增益约为 4 dB 至 6 dB

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于纹理变换网络（TTSR）的光场超分辨率方法，通过三个模块协同提升光场图像的空间分辨率。
AI.motivation: 手持光场相机因空间角分辨率权衡导致空间分辨率不足，需借助深度学习方法突破硬件限制。
AI.method: 方法包含三阶段：1) 生成全聚焦参考图像；2) 通过TTSR网络生成高分辨率光场；3) 利用光场先验优化空间分辨率。
AI.result: 实验显示该方法在PSNR指标上较双三次插值提升4-6dB，显著优于传统方法。
AI.conclusion: 提出的多模块协同框架有效解决了光场超分辨率难题，验证了纹理变换网络与光场先验结合的可行性。

authors_text: Anwaar Ulhaq,Naveed Akhtar
title: Efficient Diffusion Models for Vision: A Survey
title_zh: 有效的视觉扩散模型：一项调查
abstract: Diffusion Models (DMs) have demonstrated state-of-the-art performance in content generation without requiring adversarial training. These models are trained using a two-step process. First, a forward - diffusion - process gradually adds noise to a datum (usually an image). Then, a backward - reverse diffusion - process gradually removes the noise to turn it into a sample of the target distribution being modelled. DMs are inspired by non-equilibrium thermodynamics and have inherent high computational complexity. Due to the frequent function evaluations and gradient calculations in high-dimensional spaces, these models incur considerable computational overhead during both training and inference stages. This can not only preclude the democratization of diffusion-based modelling, but also hinder the adaption of diffusion models in real-life applications. Not to mention, the efficiency of computational models is fast becoming a significant concern due to excessive energy consumption and environmental scares. These factors have led to multiple contributions in the literature that focus on devising computationally efficient DMs. In this review, we present the most recent advances in diffusion models for vision, specifically focusing on the important design aspects that affect the computational efficiency of DMs. In particular, we emphasize the recently proposed design choices that have led to more efficient DMs. Unlike the other recent reviews, which discuss diffusion models from a broad perspective, this survey is aimed at pushing this research direction forward by highlighting the design strategies in the literature that are resulting in practicable models for the broader research community. We also provide a future outlook of diffusion models in vision from their computational efficiency viewpoint.
summary: Diffusion Models (DMs) have demonstrated state-of-the-art performance in content generation without requiring adversarial training. These models are trained using a two-step process. First, a forward - diffusion - process gradually adds noise to a datum (usually an image). Then, a backward - reverse diffusion - process gradually removes the noise to turn it into a sample of the target distribution being modelled. DMs are inspired by non-equilibrium thermodynamics and have inherent high computational complexity. Due to the frequent function evaluations and gradient calculations in high-dimensional spaces, these models incur considerable computational overhead during both training and inference stages. This can not only preclude the democratization of diffusion-based modelling, but also hinder the adaption of diffusion models in real-life applications. Not to mention, the efficiency of computational models is fast becoming a significant concern due to excessive energy consumption and environmental scares. These factors have led to multiple contributions in the literature that focus on devising computationally efficient DMs. In this review, we present the most recent advances in diffusion models for vision, specifically focusing on the important design aspects that affect the computational efficiency of DMs. In particular, we emphasize the recently proposed design choices that have led to more efficient DMs. Unlike the other recent reviews, which discuss diffusion models from a broad perspective, this survey is aimed at pushing this research direction forward by highlighting the design strategies in the literature that are resulting in practicable models for the broader research community. We also provide a future outlook of diffusion models in vision from their computational efficiency viewpoint.
abstract_zh: 扩散模型（DM）已经在内容生成方面展示了最先进的性能，而无需对抗性训练。这些模型使用两步过程进行训练。首先，前向扩散过程逐渐向数据（通常是图像）添加噪声。然后，后向扩散过程逐渐消除噪声，将其转变为正在建模的目标分布的样本。 DM 受到非平衡热力学的启发，具有固有的高计算复杂性。由于高维空间中频繁的函数评估和梯度计算，这些模型在训练和推理阶段都会产生相当大的计算开销。这不仅会阻碍基于扩散的建模的民主化，还会阻碍扩散模型在现实生活中的应用。更不用说，由于过度的能源消耗和环境担忧，计算模型的效率正迅速成为一个重要问题。这些因素导致文献中出现了多项致力于设计计算高效的 DM 的贡献。在这篇综述中，我们介绍了视觉扩散模型的最新进展，特别关注影响 DM 计算效率的重要设计方面。我们特别强调最近提出的设计选择，这些设计选择导致了更高效的 DM。与最近从广泛的角度讨论扩散模型的其他评论不同，本次调查旨在通过强调文献中的设计策略来推动这一研究方向，这些策略为更广泛的研究界带来了实用的模型。我们还从计算效率的角度提供了视觉扩散模型的未来展望。

AI.tldr: 本文综述了视觉领域中高效扩散模型的最新进展，重点分析影响其计算效率的设计方案。
AI.motivation: 扩散模型因高计算复杂度和能耗问题限制了其实际应用，亟需提升计算效率以推动技术普及。
AI.method: 通过文献回顾，系统梳理并对比了提升扩散模型计算效率的关键设计策略，聚焦近期创新方案。
AI.result: 总结了高效扩散模型的核心设计思路，为研究社区提供了可落地的优化方向与实践参考。
AI.conclusion: 计算效率是扩散模型发展的关键，未来需持续探索兼顾性能与效率的创新架构。

authors_text: Benedikte Wallace,Charles P. Martin
title: Embodying the Glitch: Perspectives on Generative AI in Dance Practice
title_zh: 体现故障：舞蹈实践中生成式人工智能的视角
abstract: What role does the break from realism play in the potential for generative artificial intelligence as a creative tool? Through exploration of glitch, we examine the prospective value of these artefacts in creative practice. This paper describes findings from an exploration of AI-generated "mistakes" when using movement produced by a generative deep learning model as an inspiration source in dance composition.
summary: What role does the break from realism play in the potential for generative artificial intelligence as a creative tool? Through exploration of glitch, we examine the prospective value of these artefacts in creative practice. This paper describes findings from an exploration of AI-generated "mistakes" when using movement produced by a generative deep learning model as an inspiration source in dance composition.
abstract_zh: 脱离现实主义对于生成人工智能作为创造性工具的潜力有何作用？通过对故障的探索，我们检验了这些文物在创作实践中的预期价值。本文描述了在使用生成深度学习模型产生的动作作为舞蹈创作的灵感来源时，人工智能生成的“错误”的探索结果。

AI.tldr: 探讨生成式AI在舞蹈创作中利用‘故障’作为创意元素的潜力
AI.motivation: 挑战传统AI生成的完美性，探索故障如何激发新的艺术表达和创作可能性
AI.method: 通过分析生成式深度学习模型产生的动作‘故障’，作为舞蹈创作的灵感来源进行实验
AI.result: 发现AI生成的故障在舞蹈中创造了独特的表现力，拓展了创作的可能性
AI.conclusion: 故障作为创意资源，为AI与舞蹈的结合提供了新视角，强调技术缺陷在艺术创作中的潜在价值

ai_content_json.result: 发现AI生成的故障在舞蹈中创造了独特的表现力，拓展了创作的可能性
ai_content_json.conclusion: 故障作为创意资源，为AI与舞蹈的结合提供了新视角，强调技术缺陷在艺术创作中的潜在价值

search_text: Embodying the Glitch: Perspectives on Generative AI in Dance Practice 体现故障：舞蹈实践中生成式人工智能的视角 What role does the break from realism play in the potential for generative artificial intelligence as a creative tool? Through exploration of glitch, we examine the prospective value of these artefacts in creative practice. This paper describes findings from an exploration of AI-generated "mistakes" when using movement produced by a generative deep learning model as an inspiration source in dance composition. 脱离现实主义对于生成人工智能作为创造性工具的潜力有何作用？通过对故障的探索，我们检验了这些文物在创作实践中的预期价值。本文描述了在使用生成深度学习模型产生的动作作为舞蹈创作的灵感来源时，人工智能生成的“错误”的探索结果。 探讨生成式AI在舞蹈创作中利用‘故障’作为创意元素的潜力 Benedikte Wallace,Charles P. Martin cs.HC

authors_text: Sahil Faizal
title: Automated Identification of Tree Species by Bark Texture Classification Using Convolutional Neural Networks
title_zh: 使用卷积神经网络通过树皮纹理分类自动识别树种
abstract: Identification of tree species plays a key role in forestry related tasks like forest conservation, disease diagnosis and plant production. There had been a debate regarding the part of the tree to be used for differentiation, whether it should be leaves, fruits, flowers or bark. Studies have proven that bark is of utmost importance as it will be present despite seasonal variations and provides a characteristic identity to a tree by variations in the structure. In this paper, a deep learning based approach is presented by leveraging the method of computer vision to classify 50 tree species, on the basis of bark texture using the BarkVN-50 dataset. This is the maximum number of trees being considered for bark classification till now. A convolutional neural network(CNN), ResNet101 has been implemented using transfer-learning based technique of fine tuning to maximise the model performance. The model produced an overall accuracy of >94% during the evaluation. The performance validation has been done using K-Fold Cross Validation and by testing on unseen data collected from the Internet, this proved the model's generalization capability for real-world uses.
summary: Identification of tree species plays a key role in forestry related tasks like forest conservation, disease diagnosis and plant production. There had been a debate regarding the part of the tree to be used for differentiation, whether it should be leaves, fruits, flowers or bark. Studies have proven that bark is of utmost importance as it will be present despite seasonal variations and provides a characteristic identity to a tree by variations in the structure. In this paper, a deep learning based approach is presented by leveraging the method of computer vision to classify 50 tree species, on the basis of bark texture using the BarkVN-50 dataset. This is the maximum number of trees being considered for bark classification till now. A convolutional neural network(CNN), ResNet101 has been implemented using transfer-learning based technique of fine tuning to maximise the model performance. The model produced an overall accuracy of >94% during the evaluation. The performance validation has been done using K-Fold Cross Validation and by testing on unseen data collected from the Internet, this proved the model's generalization capability for real-world uses.
abstract_zh: 树种识别在森林保护、疾病诊断和植物生产等林业相关任务中发挥着关键作用。关于树的哪一部分用于区分，是否应该是叶子、果实、花朵还是树皮，一直存在争论。研究证明，树皮至关重要，因为尽管季节变化，树皮仍然存在，并通过结构的变化为树木提供了特征。本文提出了一种基于深度学习的方法，利用计算机视觉方法，根据 BarkVN-50 数据集的树皮纹理对 50 种树种进行分类。这是迄今为止考虑进行树皮分类的最大树木数量。卷积神经网络 (CNN) ResNet101 使用基于迁移学习的微调技术来实现，以最大限度地提高模型性能。该模型在评估过程中的总体准确率>94%。性能验证是使用 K-Fold 交叉验证完成的，并通过对从互联网收集的未见过的数据进行测试，证明了该模型对现实世界用途的泛化能力。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于卷积神经网络（CNN）的树种自动识别方法，通过树皮纹理分类实现50种树种的识别。
AI.motivation: 树种识别在林业中至关重要，而树皮因不受季节影响且具有独特纹理特征，成为比叶片、果实等更可靠的识别依据。
AI.method: 采用ResNet101卷积神经网络，结合迁移学习中的微调技术，在包含50种树种的BarkVN-50数据集上训练模型，并通过K折交叉验证和互联网未见数据测试验证泛化能力。
AI.result: 模型在评估中达到超过94%的总体准确率，验证了其在真实场景中的泛化能力。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过树皮纹理分类有效实现了高精度树种识别，为林业应用提供了可靠的技术方案。

authors_text: Bahjat Kawar,Shiran Zada,Oran Lang,Omer Tov,Huiwen Chang,Tali Dekel,Inbar Mosseri,Michal Irani
title: Imagic: Text-Based Real Image Editing with Diffusion Models
title_zh: Imagic：使用扩散模型进行基于文本的真实图像编辑
abstract: Text-conditioned image editing has recently attracted considerable interest. However, most methods are currently either limited to specific editing types (e.g., object overlay, style transfer), or apply to synthetically generated images, or require multiple input images of a common object. In this paper we demonstrate, for the very first time, the ability to apply complex (e.g., non-rigid) text-guided semantic edits to a single real image. For example, we can change the posture and composition of one or multiple objects inside an image, while preserving its original characteristics. Our method can make a standing dog sit down or jump, cause a bird to spread its wings, etc. -- each within its single high-resolution natural image provided by the user. Contrary to previous work, our proposed method requires only a single input image and a target text (the desired edit). It operates on real images, and does not require any additional inputs (such as image masks or additional views of the object). Our method, which we call "Imagic", leverages a pre-trained text-to-image diffusion model for this task. It produces a text embedding that aligns with both the input image and the target text, while fine-tuning the diffusion model to capture the image-specific appearance. We demonstrate the quality and versatility of our method on numerous inputs from various domains, showcasing a plethora of high quality complex semantic image edits, all within a single unified framework.
summary: Text-conditioned image editing has recently attracted considerable interest. However, most methods are currently either limited to specific editing types (e.g., object overlay, style transfer), or apply to synthetically generated images, or require multiple input images of a common object. In this paper we demonstrate, for the very first time, the ability to apply complex (e.g., non-rigid) text-guided semantic edits to a single real image. For example, we can change the posture and composition of one or multiple objects inside an image, while preserving its original characteristics. Our method can make a standing dog sit down or jump, cause a bird to spread its wings, etc. -- each within its single high-resolution natural image provided by the user. Contrary to previous work, our proposed method requires only a single input image and a target text (the desired edit). It operates on real images, and does not require any additional inputs (such as image masks or additional views of the object). Our method, which we call "Imagic", leverages a pre-trained text-to-image diffusion model for this task. It produces a text embedding that aligns with both the input image and the target text, while fine-tuning the diffusion model to capture the image-specific appearance. We demonstrate the quality and versatility of our method on numerous inputs from various domains, showcasing a plethora of high quality complex semantic image edits, all within a single unified framework.
abstract_zh: 文本条件图像编辑最近引起了相当大的兴趣。然而，大多数方法目前要么仅限于特定的编辑类型（例如，对象叠加、风格转换），要么适用于合成生成的图像，要么需要公共对象的多个输入图像。在本文中，我们首次展示了将复杂（例如，非刚性）文本引导语义编辑应用​​于单个真实图像的能力。例如，我们可以改变图像内一个或多个对象的姿势和构图，同时保留其原始特征。我们的方法可以让一只站立的狗坐下或跳跃，让一只鸟展开翅膀等等——每一个都在用户提供的单个高分辨率自然图像中。与之前的工作相反，我们提出的方法仅需要单个输入图像和目标文本（所需的编辑）。它对真实图像进行操作，不需要任何额外的输入（例如图像蒙版或对象的附加视图）。我们的方法称为“Imagic”，利用预先训练的文本到图像扩散模型来完成此任务。它生成与输入图像和目标文本对齐的文本嵌入，同时微调扩散模型以捕获图像特定的外观。我们在来自不同领域的大量输入上展示了我们方法的质量和多功能性，展示了大量高质量的复杂语义图像编辑，所有这些都在一个统一的框架内。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于文本的单张真实图像编辑方法Imagic，能够通过扩散模型实现复杂的语义编辑。
AI.motivation: 现有文本条件图像编辑方法存在编辑类型受限、依赖合成图像或多视角输入等不足，难以直接对单张真实图像进行复杂语义修改。
AI.method: 利用预训练文本到图像扩散模型，通过生成与输入图像和目标文本对齐的文本嵌入，并微调扩散模型以捕捉图像特定外观，实现单图编辑。
AI.result: 在多个领域数据集上展示了高质量的复杂语义编辑效果，如改变动物姿态、物体组合等，无需额外输入（如掩码或多视角图像）。
AI.conclusion: Imagic验证了基于扩散模型的单图文本驱动编辑可行性，为真实场景图像编辑提供了统一高效的解决方案。

authors_text: Jyh-Jing Hwang,Henrik Kretzschmar,Joshua Manela,Sean Rafferty,Nicholas Armstrong-Crews,Tiffany Chen,Dragomir Anguelov
title: CramNet: Camera-Radar Fusion with Ray-Constrained Cross-Attention for Robust 3D Object Detection
title_zh: CramNet：具有光线约束交叉注意力的相机雷达融合，用于稳健的 3D 物体检测
abstract: Robust 3D object detection is critical for safe autonomous driving. Camera and radar sensors are synergistic as they capture complementary information and work well under different environmental conditions. Fusing camera and radar data is challenging, however, as each of the sensors lacks information along a perpendicular axis, that is, depth is unknown to camera and elevation is unknown to radar. We propose the camera-radar matching network CramNet, an efficient approach to fuse the sensor readings from camera and radar in a joint 3D space. To leverage radar range measurements for better camera depth predictions, we propose a novel ray-constrained cross-attention mechanism that resolves the ambiguity in the geometric correspondences between camera features and radar features. Our method supports training with sensor modality dropout, which leads to robust 3D object detection, even when a camera or radar sensor suddenly malfunctions on a vehicle. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our fusion approach through extensive experiments on the RADIATE dataset, one of the few large-scale datasets that provide radar radio frequency imagery. A camera-only variant of our method achieves competitive performance in monocular 3D object detection on the Waymo Open Dataset.
summary: Robust 3D object detection is critical for safe autonomous driving. Camera and radar sensors are synergistic as they capture complementary information and work well under different environmental conditions. Fusing camera and radar data is challenging, however, as each of the sensors lacks information along a perpendicular axis, that is, depth is unknown to camera and elevation is unknown to radar. We propose the camera-radar matching network CramNet, an efficient approach to fuse the sensor readings from camera and radar in a joint 3D space. To leverage radar range measurements for better camera depth predictions, we propose a novel ray-constrained cross-attention mechanism that resolves the ambiguity in the geometric correspondences between camera features and radar features. Our method supports training with sensor modality dropout, which leads to robust 3D object detection, even when a camera or radar sensor suddenly malfunctions on a vehicle. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our fusion approach through extensive experiments on the RADIATE dataset, one of the few large-scale datasets that provide radar radio frequency imagery. A camera-only variant of our method achieves competitive performance in monocular 3D object detection on the Waymo Open Dataset.
abstract_zh: 强大的 3D 物体检测对于安全自动驾驶至关重要。摄像头和雷达传感器具有协同作用，因为它们捕获互补信息并在不同环境条件下正常工作。然而，融合相机和雷达数据具有挑战性，因为每个传感器都缺乏沿垂直轴的信息，即相机未知深度，雷达未知高度。我们提出了摄像头-雷达匹配网络 CramNet，这是一种在联合 3D 空间中融合摄像头和雷达传感器读数的有效方法。为了利用雷达距离测量来更好地预测相机深度，我们提出了一种新颖的光线约束交叉注意机制，可以解决相机特征和雷达特征之间几何对应的模糊性。我们的方法支持使用传感器模态丢失进行训练，即使车辆上的摄像头或雷达传感器突然发生故障，也能实现强大的 3D 对象检测。我们通过对 RADIATE 数据集进行大量实验来证明我们的融合方法的有效性，RADIATE 数据集是为数不多的提供雷达射频图像的大型数据集之一。我们方法的仅相机变体在 Waymo 开放数据集上的单目 3D 物体检测中实现了具有竞争力的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出CramNet方法，通过射线约束交叉注意力机制融合摄像头与雷达数据，实现鲁棒的3D物体检测。
AI.motivation: 摄像头与雷达传感器在自动驾驶中互补但存在信息缺失问题（摄像头无深度、雷达无高度），需解决几何对应关系歧义以提升检测鲁棒性。
AI.method: 设计CramNet网络，在联合3D空间中利用雷达数据优化摄像头深度预测，引入射线约束交叉注意力机制，并支持传感器模态丢弃训练以增强鲁棒性。
AI.result: 在RADIATE数据集上验证了融合方法的有效性，单目检测版本在Waymo Open Dataset上表现优异。
AI.conclusion: CramNet通过有效融合多传感器数据，显著提升了3D物体检测的鲁棒性，尤其在传感器故障场景下表现稳定。

authors_text: Maurizio Titz,Franz Kaiser,Johannes Kruse,Dirk Witthaut
title: Predicting Dynamic Stability from Static Features in Power Grid Models using Machine Learning
title_zh: 使用机器学习根据电网模型中的静态特征预测动态稳定性
abstract: A reliable supply with electric power is vital for our society. Transmission line failures are among the biggest threats for power grid stability as they may lead to a splitting of the grid into mutual asynchronous fragments. New conceptual methods are needed to assess system stability that complement existing simulation models. In this article we propose a combination of network science metrics and machine learning models to predict the risk of desynchronisation events. Network science provides metrics for essential properties of transmission lines such as their redundancy or centrality. Machine learning models perform inherent feature selection and thus reveal key factors that determine network robustness and vulnerability. As a case study, we train and test such models on simulated data from several synthetic test grids. We find that the integrated models are capable of predicting desynchronisation events after line failures with an average precision greater than $0.996$ when averaging over all data sets. Learning transfer between different data sets is generally possible, at a slight loss of prediction performance. Our results suggest that power grid desynchronisation is essentially governed by only a few network metrics that quantify the networks ability to reroute flow without creating exceedingly high static line loadings.
summary: A reliable supply with electric power is vital for our society. Transmission line failures are among the biggest threats for power grid stability as they may lead to a splitting of the grid into mutual asynchronous fragments. New conceptual methods are needed to assess system stability that complement existing simulation models. In this article we propose a combination of network science metrics and machine learning models to predict the risk of desynchronisation events. Network science provides metrics for essential properties of transmission lines such as their redundancy or centrality. Machine learning models perform inherent feature selection and thus reveal key factors that determine network robustness and vulnerability. As a case study, we train and test such models on simulated data from several synthetic test grids. We find that the integrated models are capable of predicting desynchronisation events after line failures with an average precision greater than $0.996$ when averaging over all data sets. Learning transfer between different data sets is generally possible, at a slight loss of prediction performance. Our results suggest that power grid desynchronisation is essentially governed by only a few network metrics that quantify the networks ability to reroute flow without creating exceedingly high static line loadings.
abstract_zh: 可靠的电力供应对我们的社会至关重要。输电线路故障是电网稳定性的最大威胁之一，因为它们可能导致电网分裂成相互异步的碎片。需要新的概念方法来评估系统稳定性，以补充现有的仿真模型。在本文中，我们提出了网络科学指标和机器学习模型的结合来预测失同步事件的风险。网络科学提供了传输线基本属性的度量，例如其冗余性或中心性。机器学习模型执行固有的特征选择，从而揭示决定网络鲁棒性和脆弱性的关键因素。作为案例研究，我们使用来自多个综合测试网格的模拟数据来训练和测试此类模型。我们发现，集成模型能够预测线路故障后的失步事件，对所有数据集进行平均时，平均精度大于 0.996 美元。不同数据集之间的学习迁移通常是可能的，但预测性能会略有损失。我们的结果表明，电网失步本质上仅由几个网络指标控制，这些指标量化了网络在不产生极高静态线路负载的情况下重新路由流量的能力。

AI.tldr: 本文提出结合网络科学指标与机器学习模型，通过静态特征预测电力网络动态稳定性。
AI.motivation: 电力系统稳定性对社会至关重要，而传输线路故障可能导致电网分裂，现有模拟模型难以有效评估此类风险，需新方法补充。
AI.method: 整合网络科学中的冗余度、中心性等指标与机器学习模型，基于合成测试电网的模拟数据训练并验证预测模型。
AI.result: 模型在平均数据集上预测解同步事件的精度超过0.996，且不同数据集间存在可迁移性，仅略有性能损失。
AI.conclusion: 电网解同步风险主要由少数网络指标决定，这些指标量化了网络在不产生过高静态负载下的流量重路由能力。

authors_text: Zhe Gan,Linjie Li,Chunyuan Li,Lijuan Wang,Zicheng Liu,Jianfeng Gao
title: Vision-Language Pre-training: Basics, Recent Advances, and Future Trends
title_zh: 视觉语言预训练：基础知识、最新进展和未来趋势
comment: A survey paper/book on Vision-Language Pre-training (102 pages)
comments: A survey paper/book on Vision-Language Pre-training (102 pages)
abstract: This paper surveys vision-language pre-training (VLP) methods for multimodal intelligence that have been developed in the last few years. We group these approaches into three categories: ($i$) VLP for image-text tasks, such as image captioning, image-text retrieval, visual question answering, and visual grounding; ($ii$) VLP for core computer vision tasks, such as (open-set) image classification, object detection, and segmentation; and ($iii$) VLP for video-text tasks, such as video captioning, video-text retrieval, and video question answering. For each category, we present a comprehensive review of state-of-the-art methods, and discuss the progress that has been made and challenges still being faced, using specific systems and models as case studies. In addition, for each category, we discuss advanced topics being actively explored in the research community, such as big foundation models, unified modeling, in-context few-shot learning, knowledge, robustness, and computer vision in the wild, to name a few.
summary: This paper surveys vision-language pre-training (VLP) methods for multimodal intelligence that have been developed in the last few years. We group these approaches into three categories: ($i$) VLP for image-text tasks, such as image captioning, image-text retrieval, visual question answering, and visual grounding; ($ii$) VLP for core computer vision tasks, such as (open-set) image classification, object detection, and segmentation; and ($iii$) VLP for video-text tasks, such as video captioning, video-text retrieval, and video question answering. For each category, we present a comprehensive review of state-of-the-art methods, and discuss the progress that has been made and challenges still being faced, using specific systems and models as case studies. In addition, for each category, we discuss advanced topics being actively explored in the research community, such as big foundation models, unified modeling, in-context few-shot learning, knowledge, robustness, and computer vision in the wild, to name a few.
abstract_zh: 本文调查了过去几年开发的多模态智能视觉语言预训练（VLP）方法。我们将这些方法分为三类：（$i$）用于图像文本任务的 VLP，例如图像字幕、图像文本检索、视觉问答和视觉基础； ($ii$) 用于核心计算机视觉任务的 VLP，例如（开放集）图像分类、对象检测和分割； ($iii$) 用于视频文本任务的 VLP，例如视频字幕、视频文本检索和视频问答。对于每个类别，我们都对最先进的方法进行了全面的回顾，并使用特定的系统和模型作为案例研究，讨论了已经取得的进展和仍然面临的挑战。此外，对于每个类别，我们都讨论了研究界正在积极探索的高级主题，例如大型基础模型、统一建模、上下文中的小样本学习、知识、鲁棒性和自然计算机视觉等等。

AI.tldr: 本文综述了近年来发展的视觉-语言预训练（VLP）方法，涵盖图像-文本、核心计算机视觉任务及视频-文本任务三大类，并探讨其进展与挑战。
AI.motivation: 随着多模态智能需求的增长，视觉-语言预训练技术成为研究热点，但缺乏系统性综述以总结现状与未来方向。
AI.method: 将VLP方法分为图像-文本任务、核心计算机视觉任务和视频-文本任务三类，每类均通过典型案例分析技术进展与挑战，并讨论前沿方向如大模型、统一建模等。
AI.result: 全面梳理了各领域的最新方法，明确了当前技术瓶颈，并指出了包括小样本学习、鲁棒性提升等在内的未来研究趋势。
AI.conclusion: VLP技术推动了多模态智能发展，但需进一步解决跨模态对齐、模型泛化能力等问题，未来研究应关注统一框架与实际应用落地。

search_text: Vision-Language Pre-training: Basics, Recent Advances, and Future Trends 视觉语言预训练：基础知识、最新进展和未来趋势 This paper surveys vision-language pre-training (VLP) methods for multimodal intelligence that have been developed in the last few years. We group these approaches into three categories: ($i$) VLP for image-text tasks, such as image captioning, image-text retrieval, visual question answering, and visual grounding; ($ii$) VLP for core computer vision tasks, such as (open-set) image classification, object detection, and segmentation; and ($iii$) VLP for video-text tasks, such as video captioning, video-text retrieval, and video question answering. For each category, we present a comprehensive review of state-of-the-art methods, and discuss the progress that has been made and challenges still being faced, using specific systems and models as case studies. In addition, for each category, we discuss advanced topics being actively explored in the research community, such as big foundation models, unified modeling, in-context few-shot learning, knowledge, robustness, and computer vision in the wild, to name a few. 本文调查了过去几年开发的多模态智能视觉语言预训练（VLP）方法。我们将这些方法分为三类：（$i$）用于图像文本任务的 VLP，例如图像字幕、图像文本检索、视觉问答和视觉基础； ($ii$) 用于核心计算机视觉任务的 VLP，例如（开放集）图像分类、对象检测和分割； ($iii$) 用于视频文本任务的 VLP，例如视频字幕、视频文本检索和视频问答。对于每个类别，我们都对最先进的方法进行了全面的回顾，并使用特定的系统和模型作为案例研究，讨论了已经取得的进展和仍然面临的挑战。此外，对于每个类别，我们都讨论了研究界正在积极探索的高级主题，例如大型基础模型、统一建模、上下文中的小样本学习、知识、鲁棒性和自然计算机视觉等等。 本文综述了近年来发展的视觉-语言预训练（VLP）方法，涵盖图像-文本、核心计算机视觉任务及视频-文本任务三大类，并探讨其进展与挑战。 Zhe Gan,Linjie Li,Chunyuan Li,Lijuan Wang,Zicheng Liu,Jianfeng Gao cs.CV,cs.CL

authors_text: Mirac Suzgun,Nathan Scales,Nathanael Schärli,Sebastian Gehrmann,Yi Tay,Hyung Won Chung,Aakanksha Chowdhery,Quoc V. Le,Ed H. Chi,Denny Zhou,Jason Wei
title: Challenging BIG-Bench Tasks and Whether Chain-of-Thought Can Solve Them
title_zh: 具有挑战性的大基准任务以及思维链能否解决它们
abstract: BIG-Bench (Srivastava et al., 2022) is a diverse evaluation suite that focuses on tasks believed to be beyond the capabilities of current language models. Language models have already made good progress on this benchmark, with the best model in the BIG-Bench paper outperforming average reported human-rater results on 65% of the BIG-Bench tasks via few-shot prompting. But on what tasks do language models fall short of average human-rater performance, and are those tasks actually unsolvable by current language models? In this work, we focus on a suite of 23 challenging BIG-Bench tasks which we call BIG-Bench Hard (BBH). These are the task for which prior language model evaluations did not outperform the average human-rater. We find that applying chain-of-thought (CoT) prompting to BBH tasks enables PaLM to surpass the average human-rater performance on 10 of the 23 tasks, and Codex (code-davinci-002) to surpass the average human-rater performance on 17 of the 23 tasks. Since many tasks in BBH require multi-step reasoning, few-shot prompting without CoT, as done in the BIG-Bench evaluations (Srivastava et al., 2022), substantially underestimates the best performance and capabilities of language models, which is better captured via CoT prompting. As further analysis, we explore the interaction between CoT and model scale on BBH, finding that CoT enables emergent task performance on several BBH tasks with otherwise flat scaling curves.
summary: BIG-Bench (Srivastava et al., 2022) is a diverse evaluation suite that focuses on tasks believed to be beyond the capabilities of current language models. Language models have already made good progress on this benchmark, with the best model in the BIG-Bench paper outperforming average reported human-rater results on 65% of the BIG-Bench tasks via few-shot prompting. But on what tasks do language models fall short of average human-rater performance, and are those tasks actually unsolvable by current language models? In this work, we focus on a suite of 23 challenging BIG-Bench tasks which we call BIG-Bench Hard (BBH). These are the task for which prior language model evaluations did not outperform the average human-rater. We find that applying chain-of-thought (CoT) prompting to BBH tasks enables PaLM to surpass the average human-rater performance on 10 of the 23 tasks, and Codex (code-davinci-002) to surpass the average human-rater performance on 17 of the 23 tasks. Since many tasks in BBH require multi-step reasoning, few-shot prompting without CoT, as done in the BIG-Bench evaluations (Srivastava et al., 2022), substantially underestimates the best performance and capabilities of language models, which is better captured via CoT prompting. As further analysis, we explore the interaction between CoT and model scale on BBH, finding that CoT enables emergent task performance on several BBH tasks with otherwise flat scaling curves.
abstract_zh: BIG-Bench（Srivastava 等人，2022）是一个多样化的评估套件，专注于被认为超出当前语言模型能力的任务。语言模型已经在这个基准测试中取得了良好的进展，BIG-Bench 论文中的最佳模型通过几次提示在 65% 的 BIG-Bench 任务中表现优于平均报告的人工评分者结果。但是，语言模型在哪些任务上低于人类评估者的平均表现，以及这些任务实际上是当前语言模型无法解决的吗？在这项工作中，我们重点关注 23 项具有挑战性的 BIG-Bench 任务，我们称之为 BIG-Bench Hard (BBH)。对于这些任务，先前的语言模型评估并没有优于平均的人类评估者。我们发现，将思想链 (CoT) 提示应用于 BBH 任务，使 PaLM 在 23 项任务中的 10 项上超过了人类评分者的平均表现，并且 Codex (code-davinci-002) 在 23 项任务中的 17 项上超过了人类评分者的平均表现。由于 BBH 中的许多任务需要多步骤推理，因此没有 CoT 的小样本提示（如 BIG-Bench 评估（Srivastava 等人，2022）中所做的那样）大大低估了语言模型的最佳性能和能力，而通过 CoT 提示可以更好地捕获这些性能和能力。作为进一步的分析，我们探索了 CoT 和 BBH 上模型规模之间的相互作用，发现 CoT 能够在多个 BBH 任务上实现紧急任务性能，而其他任务的规模曲线则平坦。

AI.tldr: 分析BIG-Bench中具有挑战性的任务，并评估Chain-of-Thought（CoT）方法是否能解决这些任务。
AI.motivation: 尽管语言模型在BIG-Bench上表现良好，但仍有部分任务未超越人类评分者，需进一步探索模型能力边界及优化方法。
AI.method: 选取23个未被模型超越的BIG-Bench任务（BBH），应用CoT提示并测试PaLM和Codex性能，同时分析CoT与模型规模的关联。
AI.result: CoT使PaLM在10/23任务、Codex在17/23任务上超越人类评分者，且CoT显著提升大规模模型在复杂任务的性能表现。
AI.conclusion: CoT提示能有效增强模型多步推理能力，传统few-shot方法低估了模型潜力，模型规模与CoT结合可实现性能突增。

search_text: Challenging BIG-Bench Tasks and Whether Chain-of-Thought Can Solve Them 具有挑战性的大基准任务以及思维链能否解决它们 BIG-Bench (Srivastava et al., 2022) is a diverse evaluation suite that focuses on tasks believed to be beyond the capabilities of current language models. Language models have already made good progress on this benchmark, with the best model in the BIG-Bench paper outperforming average reported human-rater results on 65% of the BIG-Bench tasks via few-shot prompting. But on what tasks do language models fall short of average human-rater performance, and are those tasks actually unsolvable by current language models? In this work, we focus on a suite of 23 challenging BIG-Bench tasks which we call BIG-Bench Hard (BBH). These are the task for which prior language model evaluations did not outperform the average human-rater. We find that applying chain-of-thought (CoT) prompting to BBH tasks enables PaLM to surpass the average human-rater performance on 10 of the 23 tasks, and Codex (code-davinci-002) to surpass the average human-rater performance on 17 of the 23 tasks. Since many tasks in BBH require multi-step reasoning, few-shot prompting without CoT, as done in the BIG-Bench evaluations (Srivastava et al., 2022), substantially underestimates the best performance and capabilities of language models, which is better captured via CoT prompting. As further analysis, we explore the interaction between CoT and model scale on BBH, finding that CoT enables emergent task performance on several BBH tasks with otherwise flat scaling curves.
