authors_text: Joseph A. Vincent,Mac Schwager
title: Reachable Polyhedral Marching (RPM): An Exact Analysis Tool for Deep-Learned Control Systems
title_zh: Reachable Polyhedral Marching (RPM)：深度学习控制系统的精确分析工具
comment: Submitted to IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems. arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2011.11609
comments: Submitted to IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems. arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2011.11609
abstract: Neural networks are increasingly used in robotics as policies, state transition models, state estimation models, or all of the above. With these components being learned from data, it is important to be able to analyze what behaviors were learned and how this affects closed-loop performance. In this paper we take steps toward this goal by developing methods for computing control invariant sets and regions of attraction (ROAs) of dynamical systems represented as neural networks. We focus our attention on feedforward neural networks with the rectified linear unit (ReLU) activation, which are known to implement continuous piecewise-affine (PWA) functions. We describe the Reachable Polyhedral Marching (RPM) algorithm for enumerating the affine pieces of a neural network through an incremental connected walk. We then use this algorithm to compute exact forward and backward reachable sets, from which we provide methods for computing control invariant sets and ROAs. Our approach is unique in that we find these sets incrementally, without Lyapunov-based tools. In our examples we demonstrate the ability of our approach to find non-convex control invariant sets and ROAs on tasks with learned van der Pol oscillator and pendulum models. Further, we provide an accelerated algorithm for computing ROAs that leverages the incremental and connected enumeration of affine regions that RPM provides. We show this acceleration to lead to a 15x speedup in our examples. Finally, we apply our methods to find a set of states that are stabilized by an image-based controller for an aircraft runway control problem.
summary: Neural networks are increasingly used in robotics as policies, state transition models, state estimation models, or all of the above. With these components being learned from data, it is important to be able to analyze what behaviors were learned and how this affects closed-loop performance. In this paper we take steps toward this goal by developing methods for computing control invariant sets and regions of attraction (ROAs) of dynamical systems represented as neural networks. We focus our attention on feedforward neural networks with the rectified linear unit (ReLU) activation, which are known to implement continuous piecewise-affine (PWA) functions. We describe the Reachable Polyhedral Marching (RPM) algorithm for enumerating the affine pieces of a neural network through an incremental connected walk. We then use this algorithm to compute exact forward and backward reachable sets, from which we provide methods for computing control invariant sets and ROAs. Our approach is unique in that we find these sets incrementally, without Lyapunov-based tools. In our examples we demonstrate the ability of our approach to find non-convex control invariant sets and ROAs on tasks with learned van der Pol oscillator and pendulum models. Further, we provide an accelerated algorithm for computing ROAs that leverages the incremental and connected enumeration of affine regions that RPM provides. We show this acceleration to lead to a 15x speedup in our examples. Finally, we apply our methods to find a set of states that are stabilized by an image-based controller for an aircraft runway control problem.
abstract_zh: 神经网络越来越多地在机器人技术中用作策略、状态转换模型、状态估计模型或以上所有模型。通过从数据中学习这些组件，能够分析学习到哪些行为以及这如何影响闭环性能非常重要。在本文中，我们通过开发计算以神经网络为代表的动力系统的控制不变集和吸引区域（ROA）的方法来朝着这一目标迈进。我们将注意力集中在具有修正线性单元（ReLU）激活的前馈神经网络上，众所周知，它可以实现连续分段仿射（PWA）函数。我们描述了可到达多面体行进（RPM）算法，用于通过增量连接行走枚举神经网络的仿射部分。然后，我们使用该算法来计算精确的前向和后向可达集，从中我们提供计算控制不变集和 ROA 的方法。我们的方法是独特的，因为我们增量地找到这些集合，而不需要基于李雅普诺夫的工具。在我们的示例中，我们展示了我们的方法在使用学习的范德波尔振荡器和摆模型的任务中找到非凸控制不变集和 ROA 的能力。此外，我们还提供了一种用于计算 ROA 的加速算法，该算法利用 RPM 提供的仿射区域的增量连接枚举。我们在示例中展示了这种加速可带来 15 倍的加速。最后，我们应用我们的方法来找到一组由基于图像的控制器稳定的状态，以解决飞机跑道控制问题。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为Reachable Polyhedral Marching (RPM)的算法，用于精确分析深度学习控制系统的控制不变集和吸引域。
AI.motivation: 随着神经网络在机器人控制中的广泛应用，需要分析其学习行为对闭环性能的影响，尤其是传统方法难以处理非凸区域的问题。
AI.method: 通过增量连接遍历枚举ReLU神经网络的仿射分段，计算前向和后向可达集，进而求解控制不变集和吸引域，并提出加速算法提升效率。
AI.result: 在范德波尔振子和摆模型任务中成功找到非凸控制不变集和吸引域，加速算法实现15倍提速，并应用于飞机跑道控制问题。
AI.conclusion: RPM算法为深度学习控制系统的精确分析提供了有效工具，能够高效处理非凸区域并提升实际应用中的安全性分析能力。

search_text: Reachable Polyhedral Marching (RPM): An Exact Analysis Tool for Deep-Learned Control Systems Reachable Polyhedral Marching (RPM)：深度学习控制系统的精确分析工具 Neural networks are increasingly used in robotics as policies, state transition models, state estimation models, or all of the above. With these components being learned from data, it is important to be able to analyze what behaviors were learned and how this affects closed-loop performance. In this paper we take steps toward this goal by developing methods for computing control invariant sets and regions of attraction (ROAs) of dynamical systems represented as neural networks. We focus our attention on feedforward neural networks with the rectified linear unit (ReLU) activation, which are known to implement continuous piecewise-affine (PWA) functions. We describe the Reachable Polyhedral Marching (RPM) algorithm for enumerating the affine pieces of a neural network through an incremental connected walk. We then use this algorithm to compute exact forward and backward reachable sets, from which we provide methods for computing control invariant sets and ROAs. Our approach is unique in that we find these sets incrementally, without Lyapunov-based tools. In our examples we demonstrate the ability of our approach to find non-convex control invariant sets and ROAs on tasks with learned van der Pol oscillator and pendulum models. Further, we provide an accelerated algorithm for computing ROAs that leverages the incremental and connected enumeration of affine regions that RPM provides. We show this acceleration to lead to a 15x speedup in our examples. Finally, we apply our methods to find a set of states that are stabilized by an image-based controller for an aircraft runway control problem. 神经网络越来越多地在机器人技术中用作策略、状态转换模型、状态估计模型或以上所有模型。通过从数据中学习这些组件，能够分析学习到哪些行为以及这如何影响闭环性能非常重要。在本文中，我们通过开发计算以神经网络为代表的动力系统的控制不变集和吸引区域（ROA）的方法来朝着这一目标迈进。我们将注意力集中在具有修正线性单元（ReLU）激活的前馈神经网络上，众所周知，它可以实现连续分段仿射（PWA）函数。我们描述了可到达多面体行进（RPM）算法，用于通过增量连接行走枚举神经网络的仿射部分。然后，我们使用该算法来计算精确的前向和后向可达集，从中我们提供计算控制不变集和 ROA 的方法。我们的方法是独特的，因为我们增量地找到这些集合，而不需要基于李雅普诺夫的工具。在我们的示例中，我们展示了我们的方法在使用学习的范德波尔振荡器和摆模型的任务中找到非凸控制不变集和 ROA 的能力。此外，我们还提供了一种用于计算 ROA 的加速算法，该算法利用 RPM 提供的仿射区域的增量连接枚举。我们在示例中展示了这种加速可带来 15 倍的加速。最后，我们应用我们的方法来找到一组由基于图像的控制器稳定的状态，以解决飞机跑道控制问题。 本文提出了一种名为Reachable Polyhedral Marching (RPM)的算法，用于精确分析深度学习控制系统的控制不变集和吸引域。 Joseph A. Vincent,Mac Schwager cs.LG,cs.AI,cs.RO,eess.SY

authors_text: Yitao Peng,Yihang Liu,Longzhen Yang,Lianghua He
title: Decoupling Deep Learning for Interpretable Image Recognition
title_zh: 解耦深度学习以实现可解释的图像识别
abstract: The interpretability of neural networks has recently received extensive attention. Previous prototype-based explainable networks involved prototype activation in both reasoning and interpretation processes, requiring specific explainable structures for the prototype, thus making the network less accurate as it gains interpretability. Therefore, the decoupling prototypical network (DProtoNet) was proposed to avoid this problem. This new model contains encoder, inference, and interpretation modules. As regards the encoder module, unrestricted feature masks were presented to generate expressive features and prototypes. Regarding the inference module, a multi-image prototype learning method was introduced to update prototypes so that the network can learn generalized prototypes. Finally, concerning the interpretation module, a multiple dynamic masks (MDM) decoder was suggested to explain the neural network, which generates heatmaps using the consistent activation of the original image and mask image at the detection nodes of the network. It decouples the inference and interpretation modules of a prototype-based network by avoiding the use of prototype activation to explain the network's decisions in order to simultaneously improve the accuracy and interpretability of the neural network. The multiple public general and medical datasets were tested, and the results confirmed that our method could achieve a 5% improvement in accuracy and state-of-the-art interpretability compared with previous methods.
summary: The interpretability of neural networks has recently received extensive attention. Previous prototype-based explainable networks involved prototype activation in both reasoning and interpretation processes, requiring specific explainable structures for the prototype, thus making the network less accurate as it gains interpretability. Therefore, the decoupling prototypical network (DProtoNet) was proposed to avoid this problem. This new model contains encoder, inference, and interpretation modules. As regards the encoder module, unrestricted feature masks were presented to generate expressive features and prototypes. Regarding the inference module, a multi-image prototype learning method was introduced to update prototypes so that the network can learn generalized prototypes. Finally, concerning the interpretation module, a multiple dynamic masks (MDM) decoder was suggested to explain the neural network, which generates heatmaps using the consistent activation of the original image and mask image at the detection nodes of the network. It decouples the inference and interpretation modules of a prototype-based network by avoiding the use of prototype activation to explain the network's decisions in order to simultaneously improve the accuracy and interpretability of the neural network. The multiple public general and medical datasets were tested, and the results confirmed that our method could achieve a 5% improvement in accuracy and state-of-the-art interpretability compared with previous methods.
abstract_zh: 神经网络的可解释性最近受到广泛关注。以前基于原型的可解释网络在推理和解释过程中都涉及原型激活，需要原型的特定可解释结构，从而使网络在获得可解释性时不太准确。因此，解耦原型网络（DProtoNet）被提出来避免这个问题。这个新模型包含编码器、推理和解释模块。至于编码器模块，提出了不受限制的特征掩模来生成表达特征和原型。对于推理模块，引入了多图像原型学习方法来更新原型，使网络能够学习广义原型。最后，关于解释模块，建议使用多动态掩模（MDM）解码器来解释神经网络，该网络利用网络检测节点处的原始图像和掩模图像的一致激活来生成热图。它通过避免使用原型激活来解释网络的决策，解耦基于原型的网络的推理和解释模块，从而同时提高神经网络的准确性和可解释性。对多个公共普通数据集和医学数据集进行了测试，结果证实，与之前的方法相比，我们的方法在准确性和最先进的可解释性方面可以实现 5% 的提高。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种解耦的原型网络（DProtoNet），通过分离推理与解释模块，同时提升图像识别的准确率和可解释性。
AI.motivation: 传统基于原型的可解释网络在推理和解释过程中均使用原型激活，导致结构复杂且牺牲准确性，亟需一种兼顾精度与可解释性的方法。
AI.method: 设计包含编码器、推理模块和解释模块的DProtoNet：编码器生成特征掩码以提取表达性原型；推理模块通过多图像原型学习更新通用原型；解释模块采用多动态掩码解码器生成热力图，避免直接依赖原型激活进行解释。
AI.result: 在多个公开通用及医疗数据集上，DProtoNet相较先前方法准确率提升5%，并达到最先进的可解释性水平。
AI.conclusion: 解耦推理与解释模块可有效平衡模型准确性与可解释性，为可解释深度学习提供新思路。

authors_text: Ángel de la Vega Jiménez
title: Aplicación de redes neuronales convolucionales profundas al diagnóstico asistido de la enfermedad de Alzheimer
title_zh: 深层神经元在阿尔茨海默病诊断辅助中的应用
abstract: Currently, the diagnosis of Alzheimer's disease is a complex and error-prone process. Improving this diagnosis could allow earlier detection of the disease and improve the quality of life of patients and their families. For this work, we will use 249 brain images from two modalities: PET and MRI, taken from the ADNI database, and labelled into three classes according to the degree of development of Alzheimer's disease. We propose the development of a convolutional neural network to perform the classification of these images, during which, we will study the appropriate depth of the networks for this problem, the importance of pre-processing medical images, the use of transfer learning and data augmentation techniques as tools to reduce the effects of the problem of having too little data, and the simultaneous use of multiple medical imaging modalities. We also propose the application of an evaluation method that guarantees a good degree of repeatability of the results even when using a small dataset. Following this evaluation method, our best final model, which makes use of transfer learning with COVID-19 data, achieves an accuracy d 68\%. In addition, in an independent test set, this same model achieves 70\% accuracy, a promising result given the small size of our dataset. We further conclude that augmenting the depth of the networks helps with this problem, that image pre-processing is a fundamental process to address this type of medical problem, and that the use of data augmentation and the use of pre-trained networks with images of other diseases can provide significant improvements.
summary: Currently, the diagnosis of Alzheimer's disease is a complex and error-prone process. Improving this diagnosis could allow earlier detection of the disease and improve the quality of life of patients and their families. For this work, we will use 249 brain images from two modalities: PET and MRI, taken from the ADNI database, and labelled into three classes according to the degree of development of Alzheimer's disease. We propose the development of a convolutional neural network to perform the classification of these images, during which, we will study the appropriate depth of the networks for this problem, the importance of pre-processing medical images, the use of transfer learning and data augmentation techniques as tools to reduce the effects of the problem of having too little data, and the simultaneous use of multiple medical imaging modalities. We also propose the application of an evaluation method that guarantees a good degree of repeatability of the results even when using a small dataset. Following this evaluation method, our best final model, which makes use of transfer learning with COVID-19 data, achieves an accuracy d 68\%. In addition, in an independent test set, this same model achieves 70\% accuracy, a promising result given the small size of our dataset. We further conclude that augmenting the depth of the networks helps with this problem, that image pre-processing is a fundamental process to address this type of medical problem, and that the use of data augmentation and the use of pre-trained networks with images of other diseases can provide significant improvements.
abstract_zh: 目前，阿尔茨海默病的诊断是一个复杂且容易出错的过程。改进这种诊断可以更早地发现疾病并改善患者及其家人的生活质量。在这项工作中，我们将使用来自 ADNI 数据库的 PET 和 MRI 两种模式的 249 个大脑图像，并根据阿尔茨海默病的发展程度标记为三类。我们建议开发一个卷积神经网络来对这些图像进行分类，在此期间，我们将研究该问题的网络适当深度、预处理医学图像的重要性、使用迁移学习和数据增强技术作为工具来减少数据过少问题的影响，以及同时使用多种医学成像模式。我们还建议应用一种评估方法，即使在使用小数据集时也能保证结果的良好重复性。按照这种评估方法，我们最好的最终模型利用了 COVID-19 数据的迁移学习，达到了 68% 的准确度。此外，在独立测试集中，同一模型达到了 70% 的准确率，考虑到我们的数据集规模较小，这是一个有希望的结果。我们进一步得出结论，增加网络的深度有助于解决这个问题，图像预处理是解决此类医疗问题的基本过程，并且使用数据增强和使用预训练网络与其他疾病的图像可以提供显着的改进。

AI.tldr: 本研究应用深度卷积神经网络对阿尔茨海默病进行辅助诊断，利用ADNI数据库中的PET和MRI脑部图像进行分类。
AI.motivation: 阿尔茨海默病的诊断过程复杂且易出错，改进诊断方法有助于早期发现疾病，提升患者及家庭的生活质量。
AI.method: 构建卷积神经网络模型，研究网络深度、医学图像预处理、迁移学习（使用新冠数据）、数据增强技术及多模态影像联合应用，并采用保证结果可重复性的评估方法。
AI.result: 最终模型在测试集上达到68%的准确率，在独立测试集上达到70%准确率，验证了方法的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 增加网络深度、图像预处理、数据增强及使用其他疾病预训练模型可显著提升小数据集下的诊断效果。

search_text: Aplicación de redes neuronales convolucionales profundas al diagnóstico asistido de la enfermedad de Alzheimer 深层神经元在阿尔茨海默病诊断辅助中的应用 Currently, the diagnosis of Alzheimer's disease is a complex and error-prone process. Improving this diagnosis could allow earlier detection of the disease and improve the quality of life of patients and their families. For this work, we will use 249 brain images from two modalities: PET and MRI, taken from the ADNI database, and labelled into three classes according to the degree of development of Alzheimer's disease. We propose the development of a convolutional neural network to perform the classification of these images, during which, we will study the appropriate depth of the networks for this problem, the importance of pre-processing medical images, the use of transfer learning and data augmentation techniques as tools to reduce the effects of the problem of having too little data, and the simultaneous use of multiple medical imaging modalities. We also propose the application of an evaluation method that guarantees a good degree of repeatability of the results even when using a small dataset. Following this evaluation method, our best final model, which makes use of transfer learning with COVID-19 data, achieves an accuracy d 68\%. In addition, in an independent test set, this same model achieves 70\% accuracy, a promising result given the small size of our dataset. We further conclude that augmenting the depth of the networks helps with this problem, that image pre-processing is a fundamental process to address this type of medical problem, and that the use of data augmentation and the use of pre-trained networks with images of other diseases can provide significant improvements. 目前，阿尔茨海默病的诊断是一个复杂且容易出错的过程。改进这种诊断可以更早地发现疾病并改善患者及其家人的生活质量。在这项工作中，我们将使用来自 ADNI 数据库的 PET 和 MRI 两种模式的 249 个大脑图像，并根据阿尔茨海默病的发展程度标记为三类。我们建议开发一个卷积神经网络来对这些图像进行分类，在此期间，我们将研究该问题的网络适当深度、预处理医学图像的重要性、使用迁移学习和数据增强技术作为工具来减少数据过少问题的影响，以及同时使用多种医学成像模式。我们还建议应用一种评估方法，即使在使用小数据集时也能保证结果的良好重复性。按照这种评估方法，我们最好的最终模型利用了 COVID-19 数据的迁移学习，达到了 68% 的准确度。此外，在独立测试集中，同一模型达到了 70% 的准确率，考虑到我们的数据集规模较小，这是一个有希望的结果。我们进一步得出结论，增加网络的深度有助于解决这个问题，图像预处理是解决此类医疗问题的基本过程，并且使用数据增强和使用预训练网络与其他疾病的图像可以提供显着的改进。 本研究应用深度卷积神经网络对阿尔茨海默病进行辅助诊断，利用ADNI数据库中的PET和MRI脑部图像进行分类。 Ángel de la Vega Jiménez eess.IV,cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Haoran Xu,Li Jiang,Jianxiong Li,Xianyuan Zhan
title: A Policy-Guided Imitation Approach for Offline Reinforcement Learning
title_zh: 策略引导的离线强化学习模仿方法
comment: Oral @ NeurIPS 2022; An extended version with more experiments & correct some experiment details in previous version
comments: Oral @ NeurIPS 2022; An extended version with more experiments & correct some experiment details in previous version
abstract: Offline reinforcement learning (RL) methods can generally be categorized into two types: RL-based and Imitation-based. RL-based methods could in principle enjoy out-of-distribution generalization but suffer from erroneous off-policy evaluation. Imitation-based methods avoid off-policy evaluation but are too conservative to surpass the dataset. In this study, we propose an alternative approach, inheriting the training stability of imitation-style methods while still allowing logical out-of-distribution generalization. We decompose the conventional reward-maximizing policy in offline RL into a guide-policy and an execute-policy. During training, the guide-poicy and execute-policy are learned using only data from the dataset, in a supervised and decoupled manner. During evaluation, the guide-policy guides the execute-policy by telling where it should go so that the reward can be maximized, serving as the \textit{Prophet}. By doing so, our algorithm allows \textit{state-compositionality} from the dataset, rather than \textit{action-compositionality} conducted in prior imitation-style methods. We dumb this new approach Policy-guided Offline RL (\texttt{POR}). \texttt{POR} demonstrates the state-of-the-art performance on D4RL, a standard benchmark for offline RL. We also highlight the benefits of \texttt{POR} in terms of improving with supplementary suboptimal data and easily adapting to new tasks by only changing the guide-poicy.
summary: Offline reinforcement learning (RL) methods can generally be categorized into two types: RL-based and Imitation-based. RL-based methods could in principle enjoy out-of-distribution generalization but suffer from erroneous off-policy evaluation. Imitation-based methods avoid off-policy evaluation but are too conservative to surpass the dataset. In this study, we propose an alternative approach, inheriting the training stability of imitation-style methods while still allowing logical out-of-distribution generalization. We decompose the conventional reward-maximizing policy in offline RL into a guide-policy and an execute-policy. During training, the guide-poicy and execute-policy are learned using only data from the dataset, in a supervised and decoupled manner. During evaluation, the guide-policy guides the execute-policy by telling where it should go so that the reward can be maximized, serving as the \textit{Prophet}. By doing so, our algorithm allows \textit{state-compositionality} from the dataset, rather than \textit{action-compositionality} conducted in prior imitation-style methods. We dumb this new approach Policy-guided Offline RL (\texttt{POR}). \texttt{POR} demonstrates the state-of-the-art performance on D4RL, a standard benchmark for offline RL. We also highlight the benefits of \texttt{POR} in terms of improving with supplementary suboptimal data and easily adapting to new tasks by only changing the guide-poicy.
abstract_zh: 离线强化学习（RL）方法通常可分为两种类型：基于 RL 的方法和基于模仿的方法。基于强化学习的方法原则上可以享受分布外泛化，但会遭受错误的离策略评估。基于模仿的方法避免了离策略评估，但过于保守而无法超越数据集。在本研究中，我们提出了一种替代方法，继承了模仿式方法的训练稳定性，同时仍然允许逻辑分布外泛化。我们将离线强化学习中传统的奖励最大化策略分解为指导策略和执行策略。在训练期间，仅使用数据集中的数据以监督和解耦的方式学习引导策略和执行策略。在评估过程中，引导策略通过告诉执行策略应该去哪里来指导执行策略，以使奖励最大化，充当 \textit{Prophet}。通过这样做，我们的算法允许来自数据集的 \textit{state-compositionality}，而不是在先前的模仿风格方法中进行 \textit{action-compositionality}。我们愚弄了这种新方法策略引导的离线强化学习（\texttt{POR}）。 \texttt{POR} 展示了 D4RL（离线 RL 的标准基准）上最先进的性能。我们还强调了 \texttt{POR} 在通过补充次优数据进行改进以及仅通过更改指南策略轻松适应新任务方面的好处。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于策略引导的离线强化学习方法POR，通过分离引导策略与执行策略，实现稳定训练与分布外泛化的结合。
AI.motivation: 传统离线强化学习方法存在策略评估误差或泛化能力不足的缺陷，需平衡训练稳定性与分布外适应性。
AI.method: 将原始策略分解为引导策略（决定目标状态）与执行策略（完成动作执行），通过监督学习独立训练，评估时由引导策略指导执行策略实现最大化奖励。
AI.result: POR在D4RL基准测试中达到SOTA性能，并能通过补充次优数据提升效果，且可通过修改引导策略快速适配新任务。
AI.conclusion: POR通过状态组合性机制有效融合模仿学习与强化学习优势，为离线RL提供了更灵活且稳定的解决方案。

authors_text: Umut Demir,Nazim Kemal Ure
title: A Scalable Reinforcement Learning Approach for Attack Allocation in Swarm to Swarm Engagement Problems
title_zh: 用于群对群交战问题中攻击分配的可扩展强化学习方法
abstract: In this work we propose a reinforcement learning (RL) framework that controls the density of a large-scale swarm for engaging with adversarial swarm attacks. Although there is a significant amount of existing work in applying artificial intelligence methods to swarm control, analysis of interactions between two adversarial swarms is a rather understudied area. Most of the existing work in this subject develop strategies by making hard assumptions regarding the strategy and dynamics of the adversarial swarm. Our main contribution is the formulation of the swarm to swarm engagement problem as a Markov Decision Process and development of RL algorithms that can compute engagement strategies without the knowledge of strategy/dynamics of the adversarial swarm. Simulation results show that the developed framework can handle a wide array of large-scale engagement scenarios in an efficient manner.
summary: In this work we propose a reinforcement learning (RL) framework that controls the density of a large-scale swarm for engaging with adversarial swarm attacks. Although there is a significant amount of existing work in applying artificial intelligence methods to swarm control, analysis of interactions between two adversarial swarms is a rather understudied area. Most of the existing work in this subject develop strategies by making hard assumptions regarding the strategy and dynamics of the adversarial swarm. Our main contribution is the formulation of the swarm to swarm engagement problem as a Markov Decision Process and development of RL algorithms that can compute engagement strategies without the knowledge of strategy/dynamics of the adversarial swarm. Simulation results show that the developed framework can handle a wide array of large-scale engagement scenarios in an efficient manner.
abstract_zh: 在这项工作中，我们提出了一种强化学习（RL）框架，该框架可以控制大规模群体的密度，以应对对抗性群体攻击。尽管在将人工智能方法应用于群体控制方面已有大量工作，但对两个敌对群体之间的相互作用的分析是一个研究相当不足的领域。该主题的大多数现有工作都是通过对敌对群体的策略和动态做出严格假设来制定策略。我们的主要贡献是将群体与群体交战问题制定为马尔可夫决策过程，并开发 RL 算法，该算法可以在不了解敌对群体的策略/动态的情况下计算交战策略。仿真结果表明，所开发的框架可以有效地处理各种大规模交战场景。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于强化学习的框架，用于控制大规模群组在对抗性群组攻击中的密度分配，无需预先了解敌方策略和动态。
AI.motivation: 现有研究多依赖对敌方群组策略和动态的硬性假设，而实际场景中这些信息往往不可知，需更通用的解决方案。
AI.method: 将群组对群组交战问题建模为马尔可夫决策过程，开发无需敌方策略/动态信息的强化学习算法。
AI.result: 仿真验证框架可高效处理多种大规模交战场景，表现出良好的扩展性和适应性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为对抗性群组交战提供了有效且可扩展的解决方案，为未来研究奠定基础。

search_text: A Scalable Reinforcement Learning Approach for Attack Allocation in Swarm to Swarm Engagement Problems 用于群对群交战问题中攻击分配的可扩展强化学习方法 In this work we propose a reinforcement learning (RL) framework that controls the density of a large-scale swarm for engaging with adversarial swarm attacks. Although there is a significant amount of existing work in applying artificial intelligence methods to swarm control, analysis of interactions between two adversarial swarms is a rather understudied area. Most of the existing work in this subject develop strategies by making hard assumptions regarding the strategy and dynamics of the adversarial swarm. Our main contribution is the formulation of the swarm to swarm engagement problem as a Markov Decision Process and development of RL algorithms that can compute engagement strategies without the knowledge of strategy/dynamics of the adversarial swarm. Simulation results show that the developed framework can handle a wide array of large-scale engagement scenarios in an efficient manner. 在这项工作中，我们提出了一种强化学习（RL）框架，该框架可以控制大规模群体的密度，以应对对抗性群体攻击。尽管在将人工智能方法应用于群体控制方面已有大量工作，但对两个敌对群体之间的相互作用的分析是一个研究相当不足的领域。该主题的大多数现有工作都是通过对敌对群体的策略和动态做出严格假设来制定策略。我们的主要贡献是将群体与群体交战问题制定为马尔可夫决策过程，并开发 RL 算法，该算法可以在不了解敌对群体的策略/动态的情况下计算交战策略。仿真结果表明，所开发的框架可以有效地处理各种大规模交战场景。 本文提出一种基于强化学习的框架，用于控制大规模群组在对抗性群组攻击中的密度分配，无需预先了解敌方策略和动态。 Umut Demir,Nazim Kemal Ure cs.RO,cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Omar Ali,Alexandre Bone,Caterina Accardo,Omar Belkouchi,Marc-Michel Rohe,Eric Vibert,Irene Vignon-Clementel
title: CoRe: An Automated Pipeline for The Prediction of Liver Resection Complexity from Preoperative CT Scans
title_zh: CoRe：通过术前 CT 扫描预测肝切除复杂性的自动化流程
comment: Accepted by the MIABID workshop at MICCAI 2022
comments: Accepted by the MIABID workshop at MICCAI 2022
abstract: Surgical resections are the most prevalent curative treatment for primary liver cancer. Tumors located in critical positions are known to complexify liver resections (LR). While experienced surgeons in specialized medical centers may have the necessary expertise to accurately anticipate LR complexity, and prepare accordingly, an objective method able to reproduce this behavior would have the potential to improve the standard routine of care, and avoid intra- and postoperative complications. In this article, we propose CoRe, an automated medical image processing pipeline for the prediction of postoperative LR complexity from preoperative CT scans, using imaging biomarkers. The CoRe pipeline first segments the liver, lesions, and vessels with two deep learning networks. The liver vasculature is then pruned based on a topological criterion to define the hepatic central zone (HCZ), a convex volume circumscribing the major liver vessels, from which a new imaging biomarker, BHCZ is derived. Additional biomarkers are extracted and leveraged to train and evaluate a LR complexity prediction model. An ablation study shows the HCZ-based biomarker as the central feature in predicting LR complexity. The best predictive model reaches an accuracy, F1, and AUC of 77.3, 75.4, and 84.1% respectively.

While experienced surgeons in specialized medical centers may have the necessary expertise to accurately anticipate LR complexity, and prepare accordingly, an objective method able to reproduce this behavior would have the potential to improve the standard routine of care, and avoid intra- and postoperative complications. In this article, we propose CoRe, an automated medical image processing pipeline for the prediction of postoperative LR complexity from preoperative CT scans, using imaging biomarkers. The CoRe pipeline first segments the liver, lesions, and vessels with two deep learning networks. The liver vasculature is then pruned based on a topological criterion to define the hepatic central zone (HCZ), a convex volume circumscribing the major liver vessels, from which a new imaging biomarker, BHCZ is derived. Additional biomarkers are extracted and leveraged to train and evaluate a LR complexity prediction model. An ablation study shows the HCZ-based biomarker as the central feature in predicting LR complexity. The best predictive model reaches an accuracy, F1, and AUC of 77.3, 75.4, and 84.1% respectively.
abstract_zh: 手术切除是原发性肝癌最普遍的治疗方法。众所周知，位于关键位置的肿瘤会使肝切除术 (LR) 变得复杂。虽然专业医疗中心经验丰富的外科医生可能拥有必要的专业知识来准确预测 LR 的复杂性并做出相应的准备，但能够重现这种行为的客观方法将有可能改善标准护理常规，并避免术中和术后并发症。在本文中，我们提出了 CoRe，这是一种自动化医学图像处理流程，用于使用成像生物标志物从术前 CT 扫描中预测术后 LR 复杂性。 CoRe 管道首先使用两个深度学习网络对肝脏、病变和血管进行分段。然后根据拓扑标准对肝脏脉管系统进行修剪，以定义肝中心区 (HCZ)，这是一个包围主要肝血管的凸面体积，从中衍生出新的成像生物标志物 BHCZ。提取并利用其他生物标志物来训练和评估 LR 复杂性预测模型。消融研究表明，基于 HCZ 的生物标志物是预测 LR 复杂性的核心特征。最佳预测模型的准确度、F1 和 AUC 分别达到 77.3%、75.4% 和 84.1%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出CoRe系统，通过术前CT扫描预测肝切除手术复杂性，利用影像生物标志物进行自动化分析。
AI.motivation: 肝切除手术复杂性评估依赖外科医生经验，缺乏客观方法易导致术中术后并发症，需开发自动化预测工具提升诊疗标准。
AI.method: 使用深度学习分割肝脏、病灶及血管，通过拓扑学修剪定义肝中央区（HCZ），提取BHCZ等生物标志物训练预测模型。
AI.result: 最佳模型准确率达77.3%，F1值75.4%，AUC值84.1%，HCZ相关标志物为预测核心特征。
AI.conclusion: CoRe系统通过影像生物标志物有效预测肝切除复杂性，为临床决策提供客观依据，HCZ标志物具有关键预测价值。

authors_text: Animesh Chaturvedi
title: Call Graph Evolution Analytics over a Version Series of an Evolving Software System
title_zh: 对不断发展的软件系统的版本系列的调用图演变分析
abstract: Call Graph evolution analytics can aid a software engineer when maintaining or evolving a software system. This paper proposes Call Graph Evolution Analytics to extract information from an evolving call graph ECG = CG_1, CG_2,... CG_N for their version series VS = V_1, V_2, ... V_N of an evolving software system. This is done using Call Graph Evolution Rules (CGERs) and Call Graph Evolution Subgraphs (CGESs). Similar to association rule mining, the CGERs are used to capture co-occurrences of dependencies in the system. Like subgraph patterns in a call graph, the CGESs are used to capture evolution of dependency patterns in evolving call graphs. Call graph analytics on the evolution in these patterns can identify potentially affected dependencies (or procedure calls) that need attention. The experiments are done on the evolving call graphs of 10 large evolving systems to support dependency evolution management. We also consider results from a detailed study for evolving call graphs of Maven-Core's version series.
summary: Call Graph evolution analytics can aid a software engineer when maintaining or evolving a software system. This paper proposes Call Graph Evolution Analytics to extract information from an evolving call graph ECG = CG_1, CG_2,... CG_N for their version series VS = V_1, V_2, ... V_N of an evolving software system. This is done using Call Graph Evolution Rules (CGERs) and Call Graph Evolution Subgraphs (CGESs). Similar to association rule mining, the CGERs are used to capture co-occurrences of dependencies in the system. Like subgraph patterns in a call graph, the CGESs are used to capture evolution of dependency patterns in evolving call graphs. Call graph analytics on the evolution in these patterns can identify potentially affected dependencies (or procedure calls) that need attention. The experiments are done on the evolving call graphs of 10 large evolving systems to support dependency evolution management. We also consider results from a detailed study for evolving call graphs of Maven-Core's version series.
abstract_zh: 调用图演化分析可以帮助软件工程师维护或演化软件系统。本文提出调用图演化分析，从演化的调用图 ECG = CG_1, CG_2,... CG_N 中提取信息，用于演化软件系统的版本系列 VS = V_1, V_2, ... V_N。这是使用调用图演化规则 (CGER) 和调用图演化子图 (CGES) 来完成的。与关联规则挖掘类似，CGER 用于捕获系统中依赖关系的共现。与调用图中的子图模式一样，CGES 用于捕获不断演变的调用图中依赖模式的演变。对这些模式演变的调用图分析可以识别需要注意的潜在受影响的依赖项（或过程调用）。实验在 10 个大型演化系统的演化调用图上完成，以支持依赖演化管理。我们还考虑了对 Maven-Core 版本系列的演变调用图进行详细研究的结果。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于调用图演变分析的方法，通过提取版本系列中的依赖变化规律，辅助软件维护与演化。
AI.motivation: 软件系统随版本迭代，依赖关系动态变化，传统方法难以有效追踪和分析调用图演变，导致维护成本增加。
AI.method: 提出调用图演变规则（CGERs）和调用图演变子图（CGESs）两种方法，分别用于挖掘依赖共现模式及跟踪依赖模式演变，结合10个大型系统及Maven-Core的版本调用图实验验证。
AI.result: 实验表明该方法能有效识别需关注的受影响依赖关系，支持依赖演化管理，并通过Maven-Core案例验证了方法的可行性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为软件演化中的依赖分析提供了有效工具，可帮助工程师高效识别关键变化，降低维护复杂度。

search_text: Call Graph Evolution Analytics over a Version Series of an Evolving Software System 对不断发展的软件系统的版本系列的调用图演变分析 Call Graph evolution analytics can aid a software engineer when maintaining or evolving a software system. This paper proposes Call Graph Evolution Analytics to extract information from an evolving call graph ECG = CG_1, CG_2,... CG_N for their version series VS = V_1, V_2, ... V_N of an evolving software system. This is done using Call Graph Evolution Rules (CGERs) and Call Graph Evolution Subgraphs (CGESs). Similar to association rule mining, the CGERs are used to capture co-occurrences of dependencies in the system. Like subgraph patterns in a call graph, the CGESs are used to capture evolution of dependency patterns in evolving call graphs. Call graph analytics on the evolution in these patterns can identify potentially affected dependencies (or procedure calls) that need attention. The experiments are done on the evolving call graphs of 10 large evolving systems to support dependency evolution management. We also consider results from a detailed study for evolving call graphs of Maven-Core's version series. 调用图演化分析可以帮助软件工程师维护或演化软件系统。本文提出调用图演化分析，从演化的调用图 ECG = CG_1, CG_2,... CG_N 中提取信息，用于演化软件系统的版本系列 VS = V_1, V_2, ... V_N。这是使用调用图演化规则 (CGER) 和调用图演化子图 (CGES) 来完成的。与关联规则挖掘类似，CGER 用于捕获系统中依赖关系的共现。与调用图中的子图模式一样，CGES 用于捕获不断演变的调用图中依赖模式的演变。对这些模式演变的调用图分析可以识别需要注意的潜在受影响的依赖项（或过程调用）。实验在 10 个大型演化系统的演化调用图上完成，以支持依赖演化管理。我们还考虑了对 Maven-Core 版本系列的演变调用图进行详细研究的结果。 本文提出了一种基于调用图演变分析的方法，通过提取版本系列中的依赖变化规律，辅助软件维护与演化。 Animesh Chaturvedi cs.SE,cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Panagiotis Kasnesis,Christos Chatzigeorgiou,Dimitrios G. Kogias,Charalampos Z. Patrikakis,Harris V. Georgiou,Aspasia Tzeletopoulou
title: MoRSE: Deep Learning-based Arm Gesture Recognition for Search and Rescue Operations
title_zh: MoRSE：用于搜索和救援操作的基于深度学习的手臂手势识别
comment: Accepted for presentation in the IEEE 8th World Forum on Internet of Things
comments: Accepted for presentation in the IEEE 8th World Forum on Internet of Things
abstract: Efficient and quick remote communication in search and rescue operations can be life-saving for the first responders. However, while operating on the field means of communication based on text, image and audio are not suitable for several disaster scenarios. In this paper, we present a smartwatch-based application, which utilizes a Deep Learning (DL) model, to recognize a set of predefined arm gestures, maps them into Morse code via vibrations enabling remote communication amongst first responders. The model performance was evaluated by training it using 4,200 gestures performed by 7 subjects (cross-validation) wearing a smartwatch on their dominant arm. Our DL model relies on convolutional pooling and surpasses the performance of existing DL approaches and common machine learning classifiers, obtaining gesture recognition accuracy above 95%. We conclude by discussing the results and providing future directions.
summary: Efficient and quick remote communication in search and rescue operations can be life-saving for the first responders. However, while operating on the field means of communication based on text, image and audio are not suitable for several disaster scenarios. In this paper, we present a smartwatch-based application, which utilizes a Deep Learning (DL) model, to recognize a set of predefined arm gestures, maps them into Morse code via vibrations enabling remote communication amongst first responders. The model performance was evaluated by training it using 4,200 gestures performed by 7 subjects (cross-validation) wearing a smartwatch on their dominant arm. Our DL model relies on convolutional pooling and surpasses the performance of existing DL approaches and common machine learning classifiers, obtaining gesture recognition accuracy above 95%. We conclude by discussing the results and providing future directions.
abstract_zh: 搜救行动中高效、快速的远程通信可以挽救急救人员的生命。然而，在现场操作时，基于文本、图像和音频的通信方式并不适合某些灾难场景。在本文中，我们提出了一个基于智能手表的应用程序，它利用深度学习（DL）模型来识别一组预定义的手臂手势，通过振动将它们映射为莫尔斯电码，从而实现急救人员之间的远程通信。通过使用 7 位在其优势手臂上佩戴智能手表的受试者执行的 4,200 个手势（交叉验证）对模型进行训练来评估模型性能。我们的深度学习模型依赖于卷积池，超越了现有深度学习方法和常见机器学习分类器的性能，获得了 95% 以上的手势识别准确率。我们最后讨论结果并提供未来方向。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于深度学习的智能手表应用，通过识别手臂手势并转换为摩尔斯电码振动信号，实现救援人员的远程通信。
AI.motivation: 传统文本、图像和音频通信在灾难场景中存在局限性，需开发高效可靠的远程通信方式以提升救援效率。
AI.method: 采用卷积神经网络模型，利用7名受试者佩戴智能手表采集的4200个手势数据进行交叉验证训练。
AI.result: 模型手势识别准确率超过95%，优于现有深度学习方法和传统机器学习分类器。
AI.conclusion: 验证了基于深度学习的手势识别在救援通信中的有效性，并提出未来改进方向。

authors_text: Runkai Zhao,Heng Wang,Chaoyi Zhang,Weidong Cai
title: PointNeuron: 3D Neuron Reconstruction via Geometry and Topology Learning of Point Clouds
title_zh: PointNeuron：通过点云的几何和拓扑学习进行 3D 神经元重建
abstract: Digital neuron reconstruction from 3D microscopy images is an essential technique for investigating brain connectomics and neuron morphology. Existing reconstruction frameworks use convolution-based segmentation networks to partition the neuron from noisy backgrounds before applying the tracing algorithm. The tracing results are sensitive to the raw image quality and segmentation accuracy. In this paper, we propose a novel framework for 3D neuron reconstruction. Our key idea is to use the geometric representation power of the point cloud to better explore the intrinsic structural information of neurons. Our proposed framework adopts one graph convolutional network to predict the neural skeleton points and another one to produce the connectivity of these points. We finally generate the target SWC file through the interpretation of the predicted point coordinates, radius, and connections. Evaluated on the Janelia-Fly dataset from the BigNeuron project, we show that our framework achieves competitive neuron reconstruction performance. Our geometry and topology learning of point clouds could further benefit 3D medical image analysis, such as cardiac surface reconstruction. Our code is available at https://github.com/RunkaiZhao/PointNeuron.
summary: Digital neuron reconstruction from 3D microscopy images is an essential technique for investigating brain connectomics and neuron morphology. Existing reconstruction frameworks use convolution-based segmentation networks to partition the neuron from noisy backgrounds before applying the tracing algorithm. The tracing results are sensitive to the raw image quality and segmentation accuracy. In this paper, we propose a novel framework for 3D neuron reconstruction. Our key idea is to use the geometric representation power of the point cloud to better explore the intrinsic structural information of neurons. Our proposed framework adopts one graph convolutional network to predict the neural skeleton points and another one to produce the connectivity of these points. We finally generate the target SWC file through the interpretation of the predicted point coordinates, radius, and connections. Evaluated on the Janelia-Fly dataset from the BigNeuron project, we show that our framework achieves competitive neuron reconstruction performance. Our geometry and topology learning of point clouds could further benefit 3D medical image analysis, such as cardiac surface reconstruction. Our code is available at https://github.com/RunkaiZhao/PointNeuron.
abstract_zh: 从 3D 显微图像重建数字神经元是研究大脑连接组学和神经元形态的重要技术。现有的重建框架在应用跟踪算法之前使用基于卷积的分割网络将神经元与噪声背景分开。追踪结果对原始图像质量和分割精度很敏感。在本文中，我们提出了一种用于 3D 神经元重建的新框架。我们的关键思想是利用点云的几何表示能力来更好地探索神经元的内在结构信息。我们提出的框架采用一种图卷积网络来预测神经骨架点，并采用另一种图卷积网络来产生这些点的连接性。我们最终通过对预测点坐标、半径和连接的解释生成目标 SWC 文件。对 BigNeuron 项目的 Janelia-Fly 数据集进行评估，我们表明我们的框架实现了有竞争力的神经元重建性能。我们对点云的几何和拓扑学习可以进一步有利于 3D 医学图像分析，例如心脏表面重建。我们的代码可在 https://github.com/RunkaiZhao/PointNeuron 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出PointNeuron框架，通过点云的几何与拓扑学习实现三维神经元重建。
AI.motivation: 现有方法依赖分割网络易受图像质量影响，需探索更鲁棒的神经元结构表征方式。
AI.method: 采用双图卷积网络分别预测神经骨架点坐标及连接关系，最终生成SWC格式重建结果。
AI.result: 在Janelia-Fly数据集上达到竞争力的重建效果，验证了方法有效性。
AI.conclusion: 点云几何拓扑学习方法可拓展至其他3D医学图像分析领域，如心脏表面重建。

authors_text: Jinpeng Hu,Zhihong Chen,Yang Liu,Xiang Wan,Tsung-Hui Chang
title: Improving Radiology Summarization with Radiograph and Anatomy Prompts
title_zh: 通过射线照片和解剖提示改进放射学总结
abstract: The impression is crucial for the referring physicians to grasp key information since it is concluded from the findings and reasoning of radiologists. To alleviate the workload of radiologists and reduce repetitive human labor in impression writing, many researchers have focused on automatic impression generation. However, recent works on this task mainly summarize the corresponding findings and pay less attention to the radiology images. In clinical, radiographs can provide more detailed valuable observations to enhance radiologists' impression writing, especially for complicated cases. Besides, each sentence in findings usually focuses on single anatomy, so they only need to be matched to corresponding anatomical regions instead of the whole image, which is beneficial for textual and visual features alignment. Therefore, we propose a novel anatomy-enhanced multimodal model to promote impression generation. In detail, we first construct a set of rules to extract anatomies and put these prompts into each sentence to highlight anatomy characteristics. Then, two separate encoders are applied to extract features from the radiograph and findings. Afterward, we utilize a contrastive learning module to align these two representations at the overall level and use a co-attention to fuse them at the sentence level with the help of anatomy-enhanced sentence representation. Finally, the decoder takes the fused information as the input to generate impressions. The experimental results on two benchmark datasets confirm the effectiveness of the proposed method, which achieves state-of-the-art results.
summary: The impression is crucial for the referring physicians to grasp key information since it is concluded from the findings and reasoning of radiologists. To alleviate the workload of radiologists and reduce repetitive human labor in impression writing, many researchers have focused on automatic impression generation. However, recent works on this task mainly summarize the corresponding findings and pay less attention to the radiology images. In clinical, radiographs can provide more detailed valuable observations to enhance radiologists' impression writing, especially for complicated cases. Besides, each sentence in findings usually focuses on single anatomy, so they only need to be matched to corresponding anatomical regions instead of the whole image, which is beneficial for textual and visual features alignment. Therefore, we propose a novel anatomy-enhanced multimodal model to promote impression generation. In detail, we first construct a set of rules to extract anatomies and put these prompts into each sentence to highlight anatomy characteristics. Then, two separate encoders are applied to extract features from the radiograph and findings. Afterward, we utilize a contrastive learning module to align these two representations at the overall level and use a co-attention to fuse them at the sentence level with the help of anatomy-enhanced sentence representation. Finally, the decoder takes the fused information as the input to generate impressions. The experimental results on two benchmark datasets confirm the effectiveness of the proposed method, which achieves state-of-the-art results.
abstract_zh: 这种印象对于转诊医生掌握关键信息至关重要，因为它是根据放射科医生的发现和推理得出的结论。为了减轻放射科医生的工作量并减少印象书写中的重复性人力劳动，许多研究人员将注意力集中在自动印象生成上。然而，最近关于此任务的工作主要总结相应的发现，而对放射图像的关注较少。在临床中，X光片可以提供更详细的有价值的观察结果，以增强放射科医生的印象书写，尤其是对于复杂的病例。此外，发现中的每个句子通常关注单个解剖结构，因此它们只需要与相应的解剖区域匹配，而不是整个图像，这有利于文本和视觉特征对齐。因此，我们提出了一种新颖的解剖增强多模态模型来促进印象生成。具体来说，我们首先构建一组规则来提取解剖结构，并将这些提示放入每个句子中以突出解剖特征。然后，应用两个单独的编码器从放射线照片和检查结果中提取特征。之后，我们利用对比学习模块在整体层面上对齐这两种表示，并在解剖增强的句子表示的帮助下使用共同注意力在句子级别上融合它们。最后，解码器将融合后的信息作为输入来生成印象。两个基准数据集的实验结果证实了所提出方法的有效性，达到了最先进的结果。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种结合放射影像和解剖学提示的多模态模型，用于改进放射科报告中印象部分的自动生成。
AI.motivation: 现有自动印象生成方法主要依赖文本发现内容，忽视了放射影像中的关键信息，而复杂病例中影像与解剖学特征的结合能显著提升印象准确性。
AI.method: 构建解剖学规则提取模块增强文本特征，采用双编码器分别提取影像与文本特征，通过对比学习对齐全局表示并利用解剖增强的句子表示进行句级共注意力融合，最终生成印象。
AI.result: 在两个基准数据集上实验验证，该方法在印象生成任务中达到最先进水平。

search_text: Improving Radiology Summarization with Radiograph and Anatomy Prompts 通过射线照片和解剖提示改进放射学总结 The impression is crucial for the referring physicians to grasp key information since it is concluded from the findings and reasoning of radiologists. To alleviate the workload of radiologists and reduce repetitive human labor in impression writing, many researchers have focused on automatic impression generation. However, recent works on this task mainly summarize the corresponding findings and pay less attention to the radiology images. In clinical, radiographs can provide more detailed valuable observations to enhance radiologists' impression writing, especially for complicated cases. Besides, each sentence in findings usually focuses on single anatomy, so they only need to be matched to corresponding anatomical regions instead of the whole image, which is beneficial for textual and visual features alignment. Therefore, we propose a novel anatomy-enhanced multimodal model to promote impression generation. In detail, we first construct a set of rules to extract anatomies and put these prompts into each sentence to highlight anatomy characteristics. Then, two separate encoders are applied to extract features from the radiograph and findings. Afterward, we utilize a contrastive learning module to align these two representations at the overall level and use a co-attention to fuse them at the sentence level with the help of anatomy-enhanced sentence representation. Finally, the decoder takes the fused information as the input to generate impressions. The experimental results on two benchmark datasets confirm the effectiveness of the proposed method, which achieves state-of-the-art results. 这种印象对于转诊医生掌握关键信息至关重要，因为它是根据放射科医生的发现和推理得出的结论。为了减轻放射科医生的工作量并减少印象书写中的重复性人力劳动，许多研究人员将注意力集中在自动印象生成上。然而，最近关于此任务的工作主要总结相应的发现，而对放射图像的关注较少。在临床中，X光片可以提供更详细的有价值的观察结果，以增强放射科医生的印象书写，尤其是对于复杂的病例。此外，发现中的每个句子通常关注单个解剖结构，因此它们只需要与相应的解剖区域匹配，而不是整个图像，这有利于文本和视觉特征对齐。因此，我们提出了一种新颖的解剖增强多模态模型来促进印象生成。具体来说，我们首先构建一组规则来提取解剖结构，并将这些提示放入每个句子中以突出解剖特征。然后，应用两个单独的编码器从放射线照片和检查结果中提取特征。之后，我们利用对比学习模块在整体层面上对齐这两种表示，并在解剖增强的句子表示的帮助下使用共同注意力在句子级别上融合它们。最后，解码器将融合后的信息作为输入来生成印象。两个基准数据集的实验结果证实了所提出方法的有效性，达到了最先进的结果。 本文提出一种结合放射影像和解剖学提示的多模态模型，用于改进放射科报告中印象部分的自动生成。 Jinpeng Hu,Zhihong Chen,Yang Liu,Xiang Wan,Tsung-Hui Chang cs.CV,cs.AI,cs.CL

authors_text: Qiqi Liu,Yuping Yan,Peter Ligeti,Yaochu Jin
title: A Secure Federated Data-Driven Evolutionary Multi-objective Optimization Algorithm
title_zh: 一种安全联合数据驱动的进化多目标优化算法
comment: This paper has been accepted by IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computational Intelligence journal
comments: This paper has been accepted by IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computational Intelligence journal
abstract: Data-driven evolutionary algorithms usually aim to exploit the information behind a limited amount of data to perform optimization, which have proved to be successful in solving many complex real-world optimization problems. However, most data-driven evolutionary algorithms are centralized, causing privacy and security concerns. Existing federated Bayesian algorithms and data-driven evolutionary algorithms mainly protect the raw data on each client. To address this issue, this paper proposes a secure federated data-driven evolutionary multi-objective optimization algorithm to protect both the raw data and the newly infilled solutions obtained by optimizing the acquisition function conducted on the server. We select the query points on a randomly selected client at each round of surrogate update by calculating the acquisition function values of the unobserved points on this client, thereby reducing the risk of leaking the information about the solution to be sampled. In addition, since the predicted objective values of each client may contain sensitive information, we mask the objective values with Diffie-Hellmann-based noise, and then send only the masked objective values of other clients to the selected client via the server. Since the calculation of the acquisition function also requires both the predicted objective value and the uncertainty of the prediction, the predicted mean objective and uncertainty are normalized to reduce the influence of noise. Experimental results on a set of widely used multi-objective optimization benchmarks show that the proposed algorithm can protect privacy and enhance security with only negligible sacrifice in the performance of federated data-driven evolutionary optimization.
summary: Data-driven evolutionary algorithms usually aim to exploit the information behind a limited amount of data to perform optimization, which have proved to be successful in solving many complex real-world optimization problems. However, most data-driven evolutionary algorithms are centralized, causing privacy and security concerns. To address this issue, this paper proposes a secure federated data-driven evolutionary multi-objective optimization algorithm to protect both the raw data and the newly infilled solutions obtained by optimizing the acquisition function conducted on the server. We select the query points on a randomly selected client at each round of surrogate update by calculating the acquisition function values of the unobserved points on this client, thereby reducing the risk of leaking the information about the solution to be sampled. In addition, since the predicted objective values of each client may contain sensitive information, we mask the objective values with Diffie-Hellmann-based noise, and then send only the masked objective values of other clients to the selected client via the server. Since the calculation of the acquisition function also requires both the predicted objective value and the uncertainty of the prediction, the predicted mean objective and uncertainty are normalized to reduce the influence of noise. Experimental results on a set of widely used multi-objective optimization benchmarks show that the proposed algorithm can protect privacy and enhance security with only negligible sacrifice in the performance of federated data-driven evolutionary optimization.
abstract_zh: 数据驱动的进化算法通常旨在利用有限数据背后的信息来执行优化，这已被证明可以成功解决许多复杂的现实世界优化问题。然而，大多数数据驱动的进化算法都是中心化的，导致隐私和安全问题。现有的联邦贝叶斯算法和数据驱动的进化算法主要保护每个客户端的原始数据。为了解决这个问题，本文提出了一种安全联合数据驱动的进化多目标优化算法，以保护原始数据和通过优化在服务器上进行的采集功能获得的新填充的解决方案。我们在每轮代理更新时通过计算该客户端上未观测点的获取函数值来选择随机选择的客户端上的查询点，从而降低泄漏有关待采样解的信息的风险。此外，由于每个客户端的预测目标值可能包含敏感信息，因此我们使用基于 Diffie-Hellmann 的噪声屏蔽目标值，然后通过服务器仅将其他客户端的屏蔽目标值发送到所选客户端。由于采集函数的计算还需要预测的目标值和预测的不确定性，因此对预测的平均目标值和不确定性进行归一化，以减少噪声的影响。在一组广泛使用的多目标优化基准上的实验结果表明，所提出的算法可以保护隐私并增强安全性，而联邦数据驱动的进化优化的性能损失可以忽略不计。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种安全的联邦数据驱动进化多目标优化算法，以解决隐私和安全问题。
AI.motivation: 数据驱动的进化算法在解决复杂现实优化问题中表现出色，但大多数是集中式的，存在隐私和安全问题。现有的联邦贝叶斯算法和数据驱动进化算法主要保护客户端的原始数据，但未保护服务器上优化获取函数所得的新解。
AI.method: 本文提出了一种安全的联邦数据驱动进化多目标优化算法，通过在每轮代理更新中随机选择客户端计算未观测点的获取函数值，以降低信息泄露风险。同时，使用基于迪菲-赫尔曼的噪声对每个客户端的预测目标值进行掩码，并通过服务器将其他客户端的掩码目标值发送给选定的客户端。此外，将预测均值目标和不确定性进行归一化以减少噪声影响。
AI.result: 实验结果表明，该算法在保护隐私和增强安全性的同时，仅对联邦数据驱动进化优化的性能造成微不足道的损失。
AI.conclusion: 本文提出的算法有效保护了原始数据和新解的隐私，同时保持了良好的优化性能，为联邦学习中的隐私保护提供了新思路。

authors_text: Hongkuan Shi,Zhiwei Wang,Ying Zhou,Dun Li,Xin Yang,Qiang Li
title: Bidirectional Semi-supervised Dual-branch CNN for Robust 3D Reconstruction of Stereo Endoscopic Images via Adaptive Cross and Parallel Supervisions
title_zh: 通过自适应交叉和并行监督实现立体内窥镜图像鲁棒 3D 重建的双向半监督双分支 CNN
comment: Accepted by IEEE Transactions on Medical Imaging
comments: Accepted by IEEE Transactions on Medical Imaging
abstract: Semi-supervised learning via teacher-student network can train a model effectively on a few labeled samples. It enables a student model to distill knowledge from the teacher's predictions of extra unlabeled data. However, such knowledge flow is typically unidirectional, having the performance vulnerable to the quality of teacher model. In this paper, we seek to robust 3D reconstruction of stereo endoscopic images by proposing a novel fashion of bidirectional learning between two learners, each of which can play both roles of teacher and student concurrently. Specifically, we introduce two self-supervisions, i.e., Adaptive Cross Supervision (ACS) and Adaptive Parallel Supervision (APS), to learn a dual-branch convolutional neural network. The two branches predict two different disparity probability distributions for the same position, and output their expectations as disparity values. The learned knowledge flows across branches along two directions: a cross direction (disparity guides distribution in ACS) and a parallel direction (disparity guides disparity in APS). Moreover, each branch also learns confidences to dynamically refine its provided supervisions. In ACS, the predicted disparity is softened into a unimodal distribution, and the lower the confidence, the smoother the distribution. In APS, the incorrect predictions are suppressed by lowering the weights of those with low confidence. With the adaptive bidirectional learning, the two branches enjoy well-tuned supervisions, and eventually converge on a consistent and more accurate disparity estimation. The extensive and comprehensive experimental results on four public datasets demonstrate our superior performance over other state-of-the-arts with a relative decrease of averaged disparity error by at least 9.76%.
summary: Semi-supervised learning via teacher-student network can train a model effectively on a few labeled samples. It enables a student model to distill knowledge from the teacher's predictions of extra unlabeled data. However, such knowledge flow is typically unidirectional, having the performance vulnerable to the quality of teacher model. In this paper, we seek to robust 3D reconstruction of stereo endoscopic images by proposing a novel fashion of bidirectional learning between two learners, each of which can play both roles of teacher and student concurrently. Specifically, we introduce two self-supervisions, i.e., Adaptive Cross Supervision (ACS) and Adaptive Parallel Supervision (APS), to learn a dual-branch convolutional neural network. The two branches predict two different disparity probability distributions for the same position, and output their expectations as disparity values. The learned knowledge flows across branches along two directions: a cross direction (disparity guides distribution in ACS) and a parallel direction (disparity guides disparity in APS). Moreover, each branch also learns confidences to dynamically refine its provided supervisions. In ACS, the predicted disparity is softened into a unimodal distribution, and the lower the confidence, the smoother the distribution. In APS, the incorrect predictions are suppressed by lowering the weights of those with low confidence. With the adaptive bidirectional learning, the two branches enjoy well-tuned supervisions, and eventually converge on a consistent and more accurate disparity estimation. The extensive and comprehensive experimental results on four public datasets demonstrate our superior performance over other state-of-the-arts with a relative decrease of averaged disparity error by at least 9.76%.
abstract_zh: 通过师生网络的半监督学习可以在少量标记样本上有效地训练模型。它使学生​​模型能够从教师对额外未标记数据的预测中提取知识。然而，这种知识流通常是单向的，其性能容易受到教师模型质量的影响。在本文中，我们通过提出两个学习者之间双向学习的新颖方式来寻求立体内窥镜图像的稳健 3D 重建，每个学习者都可以同时扮演教师和学生的角色。具体来说，我们引入两种自监督，即自适应交叉监督（ACS）和自适应并行监督（APS）来学习双分支卷积神经网络。两个分支预测同一位置的两种不同的视差概率分布，并将它们的期望输出为视差值。学到的知识沿着两个方向跨分支流动：交叉方向（ACS 中的视差引导分布）和平行方向（APS 中的视差引导视差）。此外，每个分支还学习信心来动态完善其提供的监督。在ACS中，预测的视差被软化为单峰分布，置信度越低，分布越平滑。在 APS 中，通过降低置信度较低的预测的权重来抑制错误预测。通过自适应双向学习，两个分支享有良好调整的监督，并最终收敛到一致且更准确的视差估计。在四个公共数据集上进行的广泛而全面的实验结果表明，我们的性能优于其他最先进的技术，平均视差误差相对降低了至少 9.76%。

AI.tldr: 该论文提出了一种基于双向半监督双分支卷积神经网络（CNN）的方法，用于立体内窥镜图像的鲁棒3D重建。
AI.motivation: 传统半监督学习中知识传递方向单一，模型性能易受教师模型质量影响，需通过双向学习提升鲁棒性。
AI.method: 设计双分支CNN，引入自适应交叉监督（ACS）和自适应并行监督（APS），通过双向知识流动优化视差估计，动态调整监督权重。
AI.result: 在四个公开数据集上实验表明，该方法相较现有技术平均视差误差降低至少9.76%。
AI.conclusion: 双向自适应监督机制有效提升了立体图像3D重建的准确性和一致性。

search_text: Bidirectional Semi-supervised Dual-branch CNN for Robust 3D Reconstruction of Stereo Endoscopic Images via Adaptive Cross and Parallel Supervisions 通过自适应交叉和并行监督实现立体内窥镜图像鲁棒 3D 重建的双向半监督双分支 CNN Semi-supervised learning via teacher-student network can train a model effectively on a few labeled samples. It enables a student model to distill knowledge from the teacher's predictions of extra unlabeled data. However, such knowledge flow is typically unidirectional, having the performance vulnerable to the quality of teacher model. In this paper, we seek to robust 3D reconstruction of stereo endoscopic images by proposing a novel fashion of bidirectional learning between two learners, each of which can play both roles of teacher and student concurrently. Specifically, we introduce two self-supervisions, i.e., Adaptive Cross Supervision (ACS) and Adaptive Parallel Supervision (APS), to learn a dual-branch convolutional neural network. The two branches predict two different disparity probability distributions for the same position, and output their expectations as disparity values. The learned knowledge flows across branches along two directions: a cross direction (disparity guides distribution in ACS) and a parallel direction (disparity guides disparity in APS). Moreover, each branch also learns confidences to dynamically refine its provided supervisions. In ACS, the predicted disparity is softened into a unimodal distribution, and the lower the confidence, the smoother the distribution. In APS, the incorrect predictions are suppressed by lowering the weights of those with low confidence. With the adaptive bidirectional learning, the two branches enjoy well-tuned supervisions, and eventually converge on a consistent and more accurate disparity estimation. The extensive and comprehensive experimental results on four public datasets demonstrate our superior performance over other state-of-the-arts with a relative decrease of averaged disparity error by at least 9.76%. 通过师生网络的半监督学习可以在少量标记样本上有效地训练模型。它使学生​​模型能够从教师对额外未标记数据的预测中提取知识。然而，这种知识流通常是单向的，其性能容易受到教师模型质量的影响。在本文中，我们通过提出两个学习者之间双向学习的新颖方式来寻求立体内窥镜图像的稳健 3D 重建，每个学习者都可以同时扮演教师和学生的角色。具体来说，我们引入两种自监督，即自适应交叉监督（ACS）和自适应并行监督（APS）来学习双分支卷积神经网络。两个分支预测同一位置的两种不同的视差概率分布，并将它们的期望输出为视差值。学到的知识沿着两个方向跨分支流动：交叉方向（ACS 中的视差引导分布）和平行方向（APS 中的视差引导视差）。此外，每个分支还学习信心来动态完善其提供的监督。在ACS中，预测的视差被软化为单峰分布，置信度越低，分布越平滑。在 APS 中，通过降低置信度较低的预测的权重来抑制错误预测。通过自适应双向学习，两个分支享有良好调整的监督，并最终收敛到一致且更准确的视差估计。在四个公共数据集上进行的广泛而全面的实验结果表明，我们的性能优于其他最先进的技术，平均视差误差相对降低了至少 9.76%。 该论文提出了一种基于双向半监督双分支卷积神经网络（CNN）的方法，用于立体内窥镜图像的鲁棒3D重建。 Hongkuan Shi,Zhiwei Wang,Ying Zhou,Dun Li,Xin Yang,Qiang Li cs.CV

authors_text: Zhihe Lu,Sen He,Da Li,Yi-Zhe Song,Tao Xiang
title: Prediction Calibration for Generalized Few-shot Semantic Segmentation
title_zh: 广义少样本语义分割的预测校准
abstract: Generalized Few-shot Semantic Segmentation (GFSS) aims to segment each image pixel into either base classes with abundant training examples or novel classes with only a handful of (e.g., 1-5) training images per class. Compared to the widely studied Few-shot Semantic Segmentation FSS, which is limited to segmenting novel classes only, GFSS is much under-studied despite being more practical. Existing approach to GFSS is based on classifier parameter fusion whereby a newly trained novel class classifier and a pre-trained base class classifier are combined to form a new classifier. As the training data is dominated by base classes, this approach is inevitably biased towards the base classes. In this work, we propose a novel Prediction Calibration Network PCN to address this problem. Instead of fusing the classifier parameters, we fuse the scores produced separately by the base and novel classifiers. To ensure that the fused scores are not biased to either the base or novel classes, a new Transformer-based calibration module is introduced. It is known that the lower-level features are useful of detecting edge information in an input image than higher-level features. Thus, we build a cross-attention module that guides the classifier's final prediction using the fused multi-level features. However, transformers are computationally demanding. Crucially, to make the proposed cross-attention module training tractable at the pixel level, this module is designed based on feature-score cross-covariance and episodically trained to be generalizable at inference time. Extensive experiments on PASCAL-$5^{i}$ and COCO-$20^{i}$ show that our PCN outperforms the state-the-the-art alternatives by large margins.
summary: Generalized Few-shot Semantic Segmentation (GFSS) aims to segment each image pixel into either base classes with abundant training examples or novel classes with only a handful of (e.g., 1-5) training images per class. Compared to the widely studied Few-shot Semantic Segmentation FSS, which is limited to segmenting novel classes only, GFSS is much under-studied despite being more practical. Existing approach to GFSS is based on classifier parameter fusion whereby a newly trained novel class classifier and a pre-trained base class classifier are combined to form a new classifier. As the training data is dominated by base classes, this approach is inevitably biased towards the base classes. In this work, we propose a novel Prediction Calibration Network PCN to address this problem. Instead of fusing the classifier parameters, we fuse the scores produced separately by the base and novel classifiers. To ensure that the fused scores are not biased to either the base or novel classes, a new Transformer-based calibration module is introduced. It is known that the lower-level features are useful of detecting edge information in an input image than higher-level features. Thus, we build a cross-attention module that guides the classifier's final prediction using the fused multi-level features. However, transformers are computationally demanding. Crucially, to make the proposed cross-attention module training tractable at the pixel level, this module is designed based on feature-score cross-covariance and episodically trained to be generalizable at inference time. Extensive experiments on PASCAL-$5^{i}$ and COCO-$20^{i}$ show that our PCN outperforms the state-the-the-art alternatives by large margins.
abstract_zh: 广义少镜头语义分割 (GFSS) 旨在将每个图像像素分割为具有丰富训练示例的基类或每个类仅有少量（例如 1-5）个训练图像的新类。与广泛研究的少镜头语义分割 FSS（仅限于分割新类）相比，GFSS 尽管更实用，但研究还远远不够。现有的 GFSS 方法基于分类器参数融合，将新训练的新颖类分类器和预训练的基类分类器组合起来形成新的分类器。由于训练数据以基类为主，这种方法不可避免地偏向基类。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种新颖的预测校准网络 PCN 来解决这个问题。我们没有融合分类器参数，而是融合了基础分类器和新分类器分别产生的分数。为了确保融合分数不偏向基类或新类，引入了一个新的基于 Transformer 的校准模块。众所周知，较低级别的特征比较高级别的特征更有助于检测输入图像中的边缘信息。因此，我们构建了一个交叉注意模块，使用融合的多级特征来指导分类器的最终预测。然而，变压器的计算要求很高。至关重要的是，为了使所提出的交叉注意模块训练在像素级别上易于处理，该模块是基于特征得分交叉协方差设计的，并经过情景训练以在推理时可推广。对 PASCAL-$5^{i}$ 和 COCO-$20^{i}$ 的广泛实验表明，我们的 PCN 大幅优于最先进的替代方案。

AI.tldr: 该论文提出了一种预测校准网络（PCN），用于解决通用小样本语义分割（GFSS）中基础类和新类分类偏差的问题。
AI.motivation: 现有GFSS方法通过融合分类器参数实现分割，但因训练数据以基础类为主，导致结果偏向基础类，无法有效处理新类。
AI.method: 提出PCN网络，通过融合基础类和新类分类器的独立得分，并引入基于Transformer的校准模块与特征-得分协方差交叉注意力机制，降低计算负担并提升泛化能力。
AI.result: 在PASCAL-5i和COCO-20i数据集上，PCN显著优于现有最先进方法，验证了其有效性。
AI.conclusion: 通过得分融合与特征校准，PCN有效缓解了分类偏差问题，为GFSS提供了更实用的解决方案。

authors_text: Nejat Anbarci,Mehmet S. Ismail
title: AI-powered mechanisms as judges: Breaking ties in chess
title_zh: 人工智能驱动的法官机制：打破国际象棋中的束缚
abstract: Recently, Artificial Intelligence (AI) technology use has been rising in sports to reach decisions of various complexity. At a relatively low complexity level, for example, major tennis tournaments replaced human line judges with Hawk-Eye Live technology to reduce staff during the COVID-19 pandemic. AI is now ready to move beyond such mundane tasks, however. A case in point and a perfect application ground is chess. To reduce the growing incidence of ties, many elite tournaments have resorted to fast chess tiebreakers. However, these tiebreakers significantly reduce the quality of games. To address this issue, we propose a novel AI-driven method for an objective tiebreaking mechanism. This method evaluates the quality of players' moves by comparing them to the optimal moves suggested by powerful chess engines. If there is a tie, the player with the higher quality measure wins the tiebreak. This approach not only enhances the fairness and integrity of the competition but also maintains the game's high standards. To show the effectiveness of our method, we apply it to a dataset comprising approximately 25,000 grandmaster moves from World Chess Championship matches spanning from 1910 to 2018, using Stockfish 16, a leading chess AI, for analysis.

At a relatively low complexity level, for example, major tennis tournaments replaced human line judges with Hawk-Eye Live technology to reduce staff during the COVID-19 pandemic. AI is now ready to move beyond such mundane tasks, however. A case in point and a perfect application ground is chess. To reduce the growing incidence of ties, many elite tournaments have resorted to fast chess tiebreakers. However, these tiebreakers significantly reduce the quality of games. To address this issue, we propose a novel AI-driven method for an objective tiebreaking mechanism. This method evaluates the quality of players' moves by comparing them to the optimal moves suggested by powerful chess engines. If there is a tie, the player with the higher quality measure wins the tiebreak. This approach not only enhances the fairness and integrity of the competition but also maintains the game's high standards. To show the effectiveness of our method, we apply it to a dataset comprising approximately 25,000 grandmaster moves from World Chess Championship matches spanning from 1910 to 2018, using Stockfish 16, a leading chess AI, for analysis.
abstract_zh: 最近，人工智能（AI）技术在体育领域的应用不断增加，以做出各种复杂的决策。例如，在复杂性相对较低的情况下，大型网球锦标赛用鹰眼实时技术取代了人类线裁判，以减少 COVID-19 大流行期间的工作人员。然而，人工智能现在已经准备好超越这些平凡的任务。一个典型的例子和一个完美的应用基础是国际象棋。为了减少日益增加的平局发生率，许多精英锦标赛都采用了快速的国际象棋决胜局。然而，这些决胜局大大降低了比赛的质量。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种新颖的人工智能驱动方法来实现客观的平局决胜机制。该方法通过将棋手的棋步与强大的国际象棋引擎建议的最佳棋步进行比较来评估棋手的棋步质量。如果出现平局，则质量指标较高的玩家赢得抢七局。这种做法不仅增强了比赛的公平性和完整性，也保持了比赛的高水准。为了展示我们方法的有效性，我们将其应用于包含 1910 年至 2018 年世界国际象棋锦标赛比赛中约 25,000 个特级大师棋步的数据集，并使用领先的国际象棋人工智能 Stockfish 16 进行分析。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于人工智能的新型围棋比赛平局判定机制，通过比较选手的落子质量与顶级棋引擎建议的最优落子来决定胜负。
AI.motivation: 当前国际象棋赛事中采用的快速对弈平局规则降低了比赛质量，亟需一种既能维持竞技水平又能保证公平性的客观判定方法。
AI.method: 使用Stockfish 16引擎分析1910-2018年间25,000个世界冠军对局的顶级棋手落子，通过计算实际落子与最优落子的偏离度构建质量评估模型。
AI.result: 实验验证该方法能有效区分选手水平，既保持了比赛严谨性又提升了判定客观性，优于传统快速对弈规则。
AI.conclusion: 人工智能驱动的平局判定机制为竞技体育提供了新的技术解决方案，可在保持比赛质量的同时实现更公平的胜负判定。

search_text: AI-powered mechanisms as judges: Breaking ties in chess 人工智能驱动的法官机制：打破国际象棋中的束缚 Recently, Artificial Intelligence (AI) technology use has been rising in sports to reach decisions of various complexity. At a relatively low complexity level, for example, major tennis tournaments replaced human line judges with Hawk-Eye Live technology to reduce staff during the COVID-19 pandemic. AI is now ready to move beyond such mundane tasks, however. A case in point and a perfect application ground is chess. To reduce the growing incidence of ties, many elite tournaments have resorted to fast chess tiebreakers. However, these tiebreakers significantly reduce the quality of games. To address this issue, we propose a novel AI-driven method for an objective tiebreaking mechanism. This method evaluates the quality of players' moves by comparing them to the optimal moves suggested by powerful chess engines. If there is a tie, the player with the higher quality measure wins the tiebreak. This approach not only enhances the fairness and integrity of the competition but also maintains the game's high standards. To show the effectiveness of our method, we apply it to a dataset comprising approximately 25,000 grandmaster moves from World Chess Championship matches spanning from 1910 to 2018, using Stockfish 16, a leading chess AI, for analysis. 最近，人工智能（AI）技术在体育领域的应用不断增加，以做出各种复杂的决策。例如，在复杂性相对较低的情况下，大型网球锦标赛用鹰眼实时技术取代了人类线裁判，以减少 COVID-19 大流行期间的工作人员。然而，人工智能现在已经准备好超越这些平凡的任务。一个典型的例子和一个完美的应用基础是国际象棋。为了减少日益增加的平局发生率，许多精英锦标赛都采用了快速的国际象棋决胜局。然而，这些决胜局大大降低了比赛的质量。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种新颖的人工智能驱动方法来实现客观的平局决胜机制。该方法通过将棋手的棋步与强大的国际象棋引擎建议的最佳棋步进行比较来评估棋手的棋步质量。如果出现平局，则质量指标较高的玩家赢得抢七局。这种做法不仅增强了比赛的公平性和完整性，也保持了比赛的高水准。为了展示我们方法的有效性，我们将其应用于包含 1910 年至 2018 年世界国际象棋锦标赛比赛中约 25,000 个特级大师棋步的数据集，并使用领先的国际象棋人工智能 Stockfish 16 进行分析。 本文提出一种基于人工智能的新型围棋比赛平局判定机制，通过比较选手的落子质量与顶级棋引擎建议的最优落子来决定胜负。 Nejat Anbarci,Mehmet S. authors_text: Ruisheng Ran,Tianyu Gao,Bin Fang
title: Transformer-based dimensionality reduction
title_zh: 基于 Transformer 的降维
abstract: Recently, Transformer is much popular and plays an important role in the fields of Machine Learning (ML), Natural Language Processing (NLP), and Computer Vision (CV), etc. In this paper, based on the Vision Transformer (ViT) model, a new dimensionality reduction (DR) model is proposed, named Transformer-DR. From data visualization, image reconstruction and face recognition, the representation ability of Transformer-DR after dimensionality reduction is studied, and it is compared with some representative DR methods to understand the difference between Transformer-DR and existing DR methods. The experimental results show that Transformer-DR is an effective dimensionality reduction method.
summary: Recently, Transformer is much popular and plays an important role in the fields of Machine Learning (ML), Natural Language Processing (NLP), and Computer Vision (CV), etc. In this paper, based on the Vision Transformer (ViT) model, a new dimensionality reduction (DR) model is proposed, named Transformer-DR. From data visualization, image reconstruction and face recognition, the representation ability of Transformer-DR after dimensionality reduction is studied, and it is compared with some representative DR methods to understand the difference between Transformer-DR and existing DR methods. The experimental results show that Transformer-DR is an effective dimensionality reduction method.
abstract_zh: 近年来，Transformer 非常流行，在机器学习（ML）、自然语言处理（NLP）和计算机视觉（CV）等领域发挥着重要作用。本文基于 Vision Transformer（ViT）模型，提出了一种新的降维（DR）模型，命名为 Transformer-DR。从数据可视化、图像重建和人脸识别等方面研究Transformer-DR降维后的表示能力，并与一些有代表性的DR方法进行比较，了解Transformer-DR与现有DR方法的区别。实验结果表明Transformer-DR是一种有效的降维方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于Vision Transformer的新型降维模型Transformer-DR，并通过多任务实验验证其有效性。
AI.motivation: Transformer在机器学习领域展现强大能力，但现有降维方法在特征表示上存在局限性，需探索更有效的降维方案。
AI.method: 基于ViT架构设计Transformer-DR模型，通过数据可视化、图像重建和人脸识别三个任务评估降维后的表征能力，并与经典降维方法对比。
AI.result: 实验结果表明Transformer-DR在多个任务中均表现出优于传统方法的特征表示能力。
AI.conclusion: Transformer-DR是一种有效的新型降维方法，能够学习到更具判别性的低维特征表示。

authors_text: Trung Xuan Pham,Jin Woong Choi,Rusty John Lloyd Mina,Thanh Nguyen,Sultan Rizky Madjid,Chang Dong Yoo
title: LAD: A Hybrid Deep Learning System for Benign Paroxysmal Positional Vertigo Disorders Diagnostic
title_zh: LAD：用于良性阵发性位置性眩晕疾病诊断的混合深度学习系统
comment: Accepted to IEEE Access 2022, 13 pages, 14 figures
comments: Accepted to IEEE Access 2022, 13 pages, 14 figures
abstract: Herein, we introduce "Look and Diagnose" (LAD), a hybrid deep learning-based system that aims to support doctors in the medical field in diagnosing effectively the Benign Paroxysmal Positional Vertigo (BPPV) disorder. Given the body postures of the patient in the Dix-Hallpike and lateral head turns test, the visual information of both eyes is captured and fed into LAD for analyzing and classifying into one of six possible disorders the patient might be suffering from. The proposed system consists of two streams: (1) an RNN-based stream that takes raw RGB images of both eyes to extract visual features and optical flow of each eye followed by ternary classification to determine left/right posterior canal (PC) or other; and (2) pupil detector stream that detects the pupil when it is classified as Non-PC and classifies the direction and strength of the beating to categorize the Non-PC types into the remaining four classes: Geotropic BPPV (left and right) and Apogeotropic BPPV (left and right). Experimental results show that with the patient's body postures, the system can accurately classify given BPPV disorder into the six types of disorders with an accuracy of 91% on the validation set. The proposed method can successfully classify disorders with an accuracy of 93% for the Posterior Canal disorder and 95% for the Geotropic and Apogeotropic disorder, paving a potential direction for research with the medical data.
summary: Herein, we introduce "Look and Diagnose" (LAD), a hybrid deep learning-based system that aims to support doctors in the medical field in diagnosing effectively the Benign Paroxysmal Positional Vertigo (BPPV) disorder. Given the body postures of the patient in the Dix-Hallpike and lateral head turns test, the visual information of both eyes is captured and fed into LAD for analyzing and classifying into one of six possible disorders the patient might be suffering from. The proposed system consists of two streams: (1) an RNN-based stream that takes raw RGB images of both eyes to extract visual features and optical flow of each eye followed by ternary classification to determine left/right posterior canal (PC) or other; and (2) pupil detector stream that detects the pupil when it is classified as Non-PC and classifies the direction and strength of the beating to categorize the Non-PC types into the remaining four classes: Geotropic BPPV (left and right) and Apogeotropic BPPV (left and right). Experimental results show that with the patient's body postures, the system can accurately classify given BPPV disorder into the six types of disorders with an accuracy of 91% on the validation set. The proposed method can successfully classify disorders with an accuracy of 93% for the Posterior Canal disorder and 95% for the Geotropic and Apogeotropic disorder, paving a potential direction for research with the medical data.
abstract_zh: 在此，我们介绍“观察与诊断”（LAD），这是一种基于深度学习的混合系统，旨在支持医学领域的医生有效诊断良性阵发性位置性眩晕（BPPV）疾病。考虑到 Dix-Hallpike 和侧头转动测试中患者的身体姿势，双眼的视觉信息被捕获并输入 LAD，用于分析和分类为患者可能患有的六种可能的疾病之一。所提出的系统由两个流组成：（1）基于 RNN 的流，获取双眼的原始 RGB 图像来提取每只眼睛的视觉特征和光流，然后进行三元分类以确定左/右后管（PC）或其他； (2) 瞳孔检测器流，当瞳孔被分类为非 PC 时检测瞳孔，并对跳动的方向和强度进行分类，以将非 PC 类型分为其余四类：向地性 BPPV（左和右）和远向性 BPPV（左和右）。实验结果表明，根据患者的身体姿势，系统可以准确地将给定的 BPPV 疾病分为六类疾病，在验证集上的准确率达到 91%。所提出的方法可以成功地对后管疾病进行疾病分类，准确率达到 93%，对向地性和逆向性疾病的准确率达到 95%，为医学数据研究奠定了潜在的方向。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于深度学习的混合系统LAD，用于通过分析患者眼动信息来诊断良性阵发性位置性眩晕（BPPV）的六种类型。
AI.motivation: BPPV的诊断依赖于医生对患者眼动的主观观察，存在误判风险。开发自动化辅助系统可提高诊断准确性和效率。
AI.method: 系统包含两个流：1）RNN流提取双眼RGB图像的视觉特征和光流信息，进行三分类（左右后半规管或其他）；2）瞳孔检测流对非后半规管病例进行分类，判断跳动方向和强度以区分地垂型和天垂型BPPV。
AI.result: 在验证集上达到91%的总体准确率，后半规管分类准确率为93%，地垂型和天垂型分类准确率分别为95%。
AI.conclusion: LAD通过整合视觉特征与瞳孔动态分析，为BPPV诊断提供了高精度的自动化解决方案，验证了医疗AI在辅助诊断中的潜力。
