authors_text: Shubhanshu Mishra,Aman Saini,Raheleh Makki,Sneha Mehta,Aria Haghighi,Ali Mollahosseini
title: TweetNERD -- End to End Entity Linking Benchmark for Tweets
title_zh: TweetNERD——推文的端到端实体链接基准
comment: 19 pages, 2 figures. Accepted to Thirty-sixth Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems Datasets and Benchmarks Track 2022. Data available at: https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.6617192 under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (CC BY 4.0) license. Check out more details at https://github.com/twitter-research/TweetNERD
comments: 19 pages, 2 figures. Accepted to Thirty-sixth Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems Datasets and Benchmarks Track 2022. Data available at: https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.6617192 under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (CC BY 4.0) license. Check out more details at https://github.com/twitter-research/TweetNERD
abstract: Named Entity Recognition and Disambiguation (NERD) systems are foundational for information retrieval, question answering, event detection, and other natural language processing (NLP) applications. We introduce TweetNERD, a dataset of 340K+ Tweets across 2010-2021, for benchmarking NERD systems on Tweets. This is the largest and most temporally diverse open sourced dataset benchmark for NERD on Tweets and can be used to facilitate research in this area. We describe evaluation setup with TweetNERD for three NERD tasks: Named Entity Recognition (NER), Entity Linking with True Spans (EL), and End to End Entity Linking (End2End); and provide performance of existing publicly available methods on specific TweetNERD splits. TweetNERD is available at: https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.6617192 under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (CC BY 4.0) license. Check out more details at https://github.com/twitter-research/TweetNERD.
summary: Named Entity Recognition and Disambiguation (NERD) systems are foundational for information retrieval, question answering, event detection, and other natural language processing (NLP) applications. We introduce TweetNERD, a dataset of 340K+ Tweets across 2010-2021, for benchmarking NERD systems on Tweets. This is the largest and most temporally diverse open sourced dataset benchmark for NERD on Tweets and can be used to facilitate research in this area. We describe evaluation setup with TweetNERD for three NERD tasks: Named Entity Recognition (NER), Entity Linking with True Spans (EL), and End to End Entity Linking (End2End); and provide performance of existing publicly available methods on specific TweetNERD splits. TweetNERD is available at: https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.6617192 under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (CC BY 4.0) license. Check out more details at https://github.com/twitter-research/TweetNERD.
abstract_zh: 命名实体识别和消歧 (NERD) 系统是信息检索、问答、事件检测和其他自然语言处理 (NLP) 应用程序的基础。我们引入了 TweetNERD，这是一个包含 2010-2021 年超过 34 万条推文的数据集，用于在推文上对 NERD 系统进行基准测试。这是推文上 NERD 最大且时间上最多样化的开源数据集基准，可用于促进该领域的研究。我们描述了 TweetNERD 对三个 NERD 任务的评估设置：命名实体识别 (NER)、真实跨度实体链接 (EL) 和端到端实体链接 (End2End)；并提供现有公开可用方法在特定 TweetNERD 分割上的性能。 TweetNERD 可在 https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.6617192 上获取，并获得知识共享署名 4.0 国际 (CC BY 4.0) 许可。请访问 https://github.com/twitter-research/TweetNERD 查看更多详细信息。

AI.tldr: 本文提出TweetNERD数据集，用于评估推文中的命名实体识别与消歧（NERD）系统，包含2010-2021年的34万+条推文。
AI.motivation: 现有NERD系统缺乏针对推文的大型、时间跨度多样化的基准数据集，限制了相关研究的发展。
AI.method: 构建包含34万+条推文的数据集，涵盖2010-2021年，并设计三种NERD任务（NER、EL、End2End）进行评估，测试现有公开方法的性能。
AI.result: 发布TweetNERD数据集（CC BY 4.0许可），提供三种任务的评估结果，验证现有方法在推文NERD任务中的表现。
AI.conclusion: TweetNERD是目前最大的开源推文NERD基准数据集，可推动相关领域研究，并提供代码和数据供进一步探索。

This is the largest and most temporally diverse open sourced dataset benchmark for NERD on Tweets and can be used to facilitate research in this area. We describe evaluation setup with TweetNERD for three NERD tasks: Named Entity Recognition (NER), Entity Linking with True Spans (EL), and End to End Entity Linking (End2End); and provide performance of existing publicly available methods on specific TweetNERD splits. TweetNERD is available at: https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.6617192 under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (CC BY 4.0) license. Check out more details at https://github.com/twitter-research/TweetNERD. 命名实体识别和消歧 (NERD) 系统是信息检索、问答、事件检测和其他自然语言处理 (NLP) 应用程序的基础。我们引入了 TweetNERD，这是一个包含 2010-2021 年超过 34 万条推文的数据集，用于在推文上对 NERD 系统进行基准测试。这是推文上 NERD 最大且时间上最多样化的开源数据集基准，可用于促进该领域的研究。我们描述了 TweetNERD 对三个 NERD 任务的评估设置：命名实体识别 (NER)、真实跨度实体链接 (EL) 和端到端实体链接 (End2End)；并提供现有公开可用方法在特定 TweetNERD 分割上的性能。 TweetNERD 可在 https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.6617192 上获取，并获得知识共享署名 4.0 国际 (CC BY 4.0) 许可。请访问 https://github.com/twitter-research/TweetNERD 查看更多详细信息。 本文提出TweetNERD数据集，用于评估推文中的命名实体识别与消歧（NERD）系统，包含2010-2021年的34万+条推文。 Shubhanshu Mishra,Aman Saini,Raheleh Makki,Sneha Mehta,Aria Haghighi,Ali Mollahosseini cs.CL,cs.AI,cs.IR,cs.LG

authors_text: Naseem Alhousani,Matteo Saveriano,Ibrahim Sevinc,Talha Abdulkuddus,Hatice Kose,Fares J. Abu-Dakka
title: Geometric Reinforcement Learning For Robotic Manipulation
title_zh: 机器人操作的几何强化学习
abstract: Reinforcement learning (RL) is a popular technique that allows an agent to learn by trial and error while interacting with a dynamic environment. The traditional Reinforcement Learning (RL) approach has been successful in learning and predicting Euclidean robotic manipulation skills such as positions, velocities, and forces. However, in robotics, it is common to encounter non-Euclidean data such as orientation or stiffness, and failing to account for their geometric nature can negatively impact learning accuracy and performance. In this paper, to address this challenge, we propose a novel framework for RL that leverages Riemannian geometry, which we call Geometric Reinforcement Learning (G-RL), to enable agents to learn robotic manipulation skills with non-Euclidean data. Specifically, G-RL utilizes the tangent space in two ways: a tangent space for parameterization and a local tangent space for mapping to a nonEuclidean manifold. The policy is learned in the parameterization tangent space, which remains constant throughout the training. The policy is then transferred to the local tangent space via parallel transport and projected onto the non-Euclidean manifold. The local tangent space changes over time to remain within the neighborhood of the current manifold point, reducing the approximation error. Therefore, by introducing a geometrically grounded pre- and post-processing step into the traditional RL pipeline, our G-RL framework enables several model-free algorithms designed for Euclidean space to learn from non-Euclidean data without modifications. Experimental results, obtained both in simulation and on a real robot, support our hypothesis that G-RL is more accurate and converges to a better solution than approximating non-Euclidean data.
summary: Reinforcement learning (RL) is a popular technique that allows an agent to learn by trial and error while interacting with a dynamic environment. The traditional Reinforcement Learning (RL) approach has been successful in learning and predicting Euclidean robotic manipulation skills such as positions, velocities, and forces. However, in robotics, it is common to encounter non-Euclidean data such as orientation or stiffness, and failing to account for their geometric nature can negatively impact learning accuracy and performance. In this paper, to address this challenge, we propose a novel framework for RL that leverages Riemannian geometry, which we call Geometric Reinforcement Learning (G-RL), to enable agents to learn robotic manipulation skills with non-Euclidean data. Specifically, G-RL utilizes the tangent space in two ways: a tangent space for parameterization and a local tangent space for mapping to a nonEuclidean manifold. The policy is learned in the parameterization tangent space, which remains constant throughout the training. The policy is then transferred to the local tangent space via parallel transport and projected onto the non-Euclidean manifold. The local tangent space changes over time to remain within the neighborhood of the current manifold point, reducing the approximation error. Therefore, by introducing a geometrically grounded pre- and post-processing step into the traditional RL pipeline, our G-RL framework enables several model-free algorithms designed for Euclidean space to learn from non-Euclidean data without modifications. Experimental results, obtained both in simulation and on a real robot, support our hypothesis that G-RL is more accurate and converges to a better solution than approximating non-Euclidean data.
abstract_zh: 强化学习（RL）是一种流行的技术，它允许代理在与动态环境交互时通过反复试验来学习。传统的强化学习 (RL) 方法在学习和预测欧几里德机器人操作技能（例如位置、速度和力）方面取得了成功。然而，在机器人技术中，经常会遇到非欧几里得数据，例如方向或刚度，如果不考虑它们的几何性质，可能会对学习准确性和性能产生负面影响。在本文中，为了应对这一挑战，我们提出了一种新的强化学习框架，该框架利用黎曼几何，我们称之为几何强化学习（G-RL），使智能体能够利用非欧几里得数据学习机器人操作技能。具体来说，G-RL 以两种方式利用切线空间：用于参数化的切线空间和用于映射到非欧几里得流形的局部切线空间。该策略是在参数化切线空间中学习的，该空间在整个训练过程中保持不变。然后，该策略通过并行传输转移到局部切线空间，并投影到非欧几里得流形上。局部切线空间随时间变化以保持在当前流形点的邻域内，从而减少近似误差。因此，通过在传统的 RL 管道中引入基于几何的预处理和后处理步骤，我们的 G-RL 框架使得为欧几里德空间设计的几种无模型算法能够从非欧几里德数据中学习而无需修改。在模拟和真实机器人上获得的实验结果支持了我们的假设，即 G-RL 比近似非欧几里得数据更准确并且收敛到更好的解决方案。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于黎曼几何的强化学习框架（G-RL），用于解决机器人操作中非欧几里得数据（如方向、刚度）的处理问题。
AI.motivation: 传统强化学习在处理非欧几里得数据时存在几何特性被忽略的问题，导致学习精度和性能下降。
AI.method: 通过引入切空间参数化和局部切空间映射，结合平行运输技术，将策略学习从固定参数化切空间转移到随时间变化的局部切空间，最终投影到非欧几里得流形。
AI.result: 仿真和真实机器人实验表明，G-RL在精度和收敛性上优于传统非欧几里得数据近似方法。
AI.conclusion: G-RL通过几何预处理和后处理步骤，使原有欧几里得空间算法无需修改即可学习非欧几里得数据，提升了机器人操作性能。

authors_text: Yangzheng Wu,Alireza Javaheri,Mohsen Zand,Michael Greenspan
title: Keypoint Cascade Voting for Point Cloud Based 6DoF Pose Estimation
title_zh: 基于点云的 6DoF 位姿估计的关键点级联投票
abstract: We propose a novel keypoint voting 6DoF object pose estimation method, which takes pure unordered point cloud geometry as input without RGB information. The proposed cascaded keypoint voting method, called RCVPose3D, is based upon a novel architecture which separates the task of semantic segmentation from that of keypoint regression, thereby increasing the effectiveness of both and improving the ultimate performance. The method also introduces a pairwise constraint in between different keypoints to the loss function when regressing the quantity for keypoint estimation, which is shown to be effective, as well as a novel Voter Confident Score which enhances both the learning and inference stages. Our proposed RCVPose3D achieves state-of-the-art performance on the Occlusion LINEMOD (74.5%) and YCB-Video (96.9%) datasets, outperforming existing pure RGB and RGB-D based methods, as well as being competitive with RGB plus point cloud methods.
summary: We propose a novel keypoint voting 6DoF object pose estimation method, which takes pure unordered point cloud geometry as input without RGB information. The proposed cascaded keypoint voting method, called RCVPose3D, is based upon a novel architecture which separates the task of semantic segmentation from that of keypoint regression, thereby increasing the effectiveness of both and improving the ultimate performance. The method also introduces a pairwise constraint in between different keypoints to the loss function when regressing the quantity for keypoint estimation, which is shown to be effective, as well as a novel Voter Confident Score which enhances both the learning and inference stages. Our proposed RCVPose3D achieves state-of-the-art performance on the Occlusion LINEMOD (74.5%) and YCB-Video (96.9%) datasets, outperforming existing pure RGB and RGB-D based methods, as well as being competitive with RGB plus point cloud methods.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种新颖的关键点投票 6DoF 物体姿态估计方法，该方法采用纯无序点云几何形状作为输入，无需 RGB 信息。所提出的级联关键点投票方法称为 RCVPose3D，基于一种新颖的架构，该架构将语义分割任务与关键点回归任务分开，从而提高了两者的有效性并提高了最终性能。该方法还在对关键点估计的数量进行回归时在不同关键点之间引入了损失函数的成对约束，这被证明是有效的，以及一种新的选民信心分数，可以增强学习和推理阶段。我们提出的 RCVPose3D 在 Occlusion LINEMOD (74.5%) 和 YCB-Video (96.9%) 数据集上实现了最先进的性能，优于现有的纯 RGB 和 RGB-D 方法，并且与 RGB 加点云方法具有竞争力。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于点云的6自由度目标姿态估计方法，无需RGB信息。
AI.motivation: 现有方法依赖RGB或RGB-D信息，而本文旨在仅使用点云数据实现更鲁棒的姿态估计。
AI.method: 提出RCVPose3D架构，分离语义分割与关键点回归任务，并引入关键点间成对约束及Voter Confident Score机制。
AI.result: 在Occlusion LINEMOD（74.5%）和YCB-Video（96.9%）数据集上达到SOTA性能，优于纯RGB/RGB-D方法且接近RGB+点云方法。
AI.conclusion: 仅依赖点云数据的姿态估计方法通过结构设计与约束机制可实现高精度，验证了纯几何信息的有效性。

authors_text: Niklas Freymuth,Nicolas Schreiber,Philipp Becker,Aleksandar Taranovic,Gerhard Neumann
title: Inferring Versatile Behavior from Demonstrations by Matching Geometric Descriptors
title_zh: 通过匹配几何描述符从演示中推断多功能行为
comment: Accepted as a poster at the 6th Conference on Robot Learning (CoRL), 2022
comments: Accepted as a poster at the 6th Conference on Robot Learning (CoRL), 2022
abstract: Humans intuitively solve tasks in versatile ways, varying their behavior in terms of trajectory-based planning and for individual steps. Thus, they can easily generalize and adapt to new and changing environments. Current Imitation Learning algorithms often only consider unimodal expert demonstrations and act in a state-action-based setting, making it difficult for them to imitate human behavior in case of versatile demonstrations. Instead, we combine a mixture of movement primitives with a distribution matching objective to learn versatile behaviors that match the expert's behavior and versatility. To facilitate generalization to novel task configurations, we do not directly match the agent's and expert's trajectory distributions but rather work with concise geometric descriptors which generalize well to unseen task configurations. We empirically validate our method on various robot tasks using versatile human demonstrations and compare to imitation learning algorithms in a state-action setting as well as a trajectory-based setting. We find that the geometric descriptors greatly help in generalizing to new task configurations and that combining them with our distribution-matching objective is crucial for representing and reproducing versatile behavior.

To facilitate generalization to novel task configurations, we do not directly match the agent's and expert's trajectory distributions but rather work with concise geometric descriptors which generalize well to unseen task configurations. We empirically validate our method on various robot tasks using versatile human demonstrations and compare to imitation learning algorithms in a state-action setting as well as a trajectory-based setting. We find that the geometric descriptors greatly help in generalizing to new task configurations and that combining them with our distribution-matching objective is crucial for representing and reproducing versatile behavior.

Thus, they can easily generalize and adapt to new and changing environments.当前的模仿学习算法通常只考虑单峰专家演示，并在基于状态-动作的设置中进行操作，这使得它们很难在多功能演示的情况下模仿人类行为。相反，我们将运动原语与分布匹配目标相结合，以学习与专家的行为和多功能性相匹配的多功能行为。为了促进对新任务配置的泛化，我们不直接匹配代理和专家的轨迹分布，而是使用简洁的几何描述符，这些描述符可以很好地泛化到未见过的任务配置。我们使用多功能人类演示在各种机器人任务上实证验证我们的方法，并与状态动作设置以及基于轨迹的设置中的模仿学习算法进行比较。我们发现几何描述符极大地有助于推广到新的任务配置，并且将它们与我们的分布匹配目标相结合对于表示和再现多功能行为至关重要。

AI.conclusion: 该方法有效捕捉人类行为的多样性，为机器人在复杂动态环境中的适应性学习提供了新思路。

search_text: Inferring Versatile Behavior from Demonstrations by Matching Geometric Descriptors 通过匹配几何描述符从演示中推断多功能行为 Humans intuitively solve tasks in versatile ways, varying their behavior in terms of trajectory-based planning and for individual steps. Thus, they can easily generalize and adapt to new and changing environments. Current Imitation Learning algorithms often only consider unimodal expert demonstrations and act in a state-action-based setting, making it difficult for them to imitate human behavior in case of versatile demonstrations. Instead, we combine a mixture of movement primitives with a distribution matching objective to learn versatile behaviors that match the expert's behavior and versatility. To facilitate generalization to novel task configurations, we do not directly match the agent's and expert's trajectory distributions but rather work with concise geometric descriptors which generalize well to unseen task configurations. We empirically validate our method on various robot tasks using versatile human demonstrations and compare to imitation learning algorithms in a state-action setting as well as a trajectory-based setting. We find that the geometric descriptors greatly help in generalizing to new task configurations and that combining them with our distribution-matching objective is crucial for representing and reproducing versatile behavior. Humans intuitively solve tasks in versatile ways, varying their behavior in terms of trajectory-based planning and for individual steps. Thus, they can easily generalize and adapt to new and changing environments.当前的模仿学习算法通常只考虑单峰专家演示，并在基于状态-动作的设置中进行操作，这使得它们很难在多功能演示的情况下模仿人类行为。相反，我们将运动原语与分布匹配目标相结合，以学习与专家的行为和多功能性相匹配的多功能行为。为了促进对新任务配置的泛化，我们不直接匹配代理和专家的轨迹分布，而是使用简洁的几何描述符，这些描述符可以很好地泛化到未见过的任务配置。我们使用多功能人类演示在各种机器人任务上实证验证我们的方法，并与状态动作设置以及基于轨迹的设置中的模仿学习算法进行比较。我们发现几何描述符极大地有助于推广到新的任务配置，并且将它们与我们的分布匹配目标相结合对于表示和再现多功能行为至关重要。 本文提出通过匹配几何描述符从多样化人类演示中学习机器人多样化行为的方法，以解决传统模仿学习在泛化能力上的不足。 Niklas Freymuth,Nicolas Schreiber,Philipp Becker,Aleksandar Taranovic,Gerhard Neumann cs.RO,cs.LG
